2299 * Call a trigger function.
2300 *
2301 *		trigdata: trigger descriptor.
2302 *		tgindx: trigger's index in finfo and instr arrays.
2303 *		finfo: array of cached trigger function call information.
2304 *		instr: optional array of EXPLAIN ANALYZE instrumentation state.
2305 *		per_tuple_context: memory context to execute the function in.
2306 *
2307 * Returns the tuple (or NULL) as returned by the function.
2308 */
2309static HeapTuple
2310ExecCallTriggerFunc(TriggerData *trigdata,
2311					int tgindx,
2312					FmgrInfo *finfo,
2313					Instrumentation *instr,
2314					MemoryContext per_tuple_context)
2315{
2316	LOCAL_FCINFO(fcinfo, 0);
2317	PgStat_FunctionCallUsage fcusage;
2318	Datum		result;
2319	MemoryContext oldContext;
2320
2321	/*
2322	 * Protect against code paths that may fail to initialize transition table
2323	 * info.
2324	 */
2325	Assert(((TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_INSERT(trigdata->tg_event) ||
2326			 TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_UPDATE(trigdata->tg_event) ||
2327			 TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_DELETE(trigdata->tg_event)) &&
2328			TRIGGER_FIRED_AFTER(trigdata->tg_event) &&
2329			!(trigdata->tg_event & AFTER_TRIGGER_DEFERRABLE) &&
2330			!(trigdata->tg_event & AFTER_TRIGGER_INITDEFERRED)) ||
2331		   (trigdata->tg_oldtable == NULL && trigdata->tg_newtable == NULL));
2332
2333	finfo += tgindx;
2334
2335	/*
2336	 * We cache fmgr lookup info, to avoid making the lookup again on each
2337	 * call.
2338	 */
2339	if (finfo->fn_oid == InvalidOid)
2340		fmgr_info(trigdata->tg_trigger->tgfoid, finfo);
2341
2342	Assert(finfo->fn_oid == trigdata->tg_trigger->tgfoid);
2343
2344	/*
2345	 * If doing EXPLAIN ANALYZE, start charging time to this trigger.
2346	 */
2347	if (instr)
2348		InstrStartNode(instr + tgindx);
2349
2350	/*
2351	 * Do the function evaluation in the per-tuple memory context, so that
2352	 * leaked memory will be reclaimed once per tuple. Note in particular that
2353	 * any new tuple created by the trigger function will live till the end of
2354	 * the tuple cycle.
2355	 */
2356	oldContext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(per_tuple_context);
2357
2358	/*
2359	 * Call the function, passing no arguments but setting a context.
2360	 */
2361	InitFunctionCallInfoData(*fcinfo, finfo, 0,
2362							 InvalidOid, (Node *) trigdata, NULL);
2363
2364	pgstat_init_function_usage(fcinfo, &fcusage);
2365
2366	MyTriggerDepth++;
2367	PG_TRY();
2368	{
2369		result = FunctionCallInvoke(fcinfo);
2370	}
2371	PG_FINALLY();
2372	{
2373		MyTriggerDepth--;
2374	}
2375	PG_END_TRY();
2376
2377	pgstat_end_function_usage(&fcusage, true);
2378
2379	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldContext);
2380
2381	/*
2382	 * Trigger protocol allows function to return a null pointer, but NOT to
2383	 * set the isnull result flag.
2384	 */
2385	if (fcinfo->isnull)
2386		ereport(ERROR,
2387				(errcode(ERRCODE_E_R_I_E_TRIGGER_PROTOCOL_VIOLATED),
2388				 errmsg("trigger function %u returned null value",
2389						fcinfo->flinfo->fn_oid)));
2390
2391	/*
2392	 * If doing EXPLAIN ANALYZE, stop charging time to this trigger, and count
2393	 * one "tuple returned" (really the number of firings).
2394	 */
2395	if (instr)
2396		InstrStopNode(instr + tgindx, 1);
2397
2398	return (HeapTuple) DatumGetPointer(result);
2399}
2400
2401void
2402ExecBSInsertTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo)
2403{
2404	TriggerDesc *trigdesc;
2405	int			i;
2406	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2407
2408	trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2409
2410	if (trigdesc == NULL)
2411		return;
2412	if (!trigdesc->trig_insert_before_statement)
2413		return;
2414
2415	/* no-op if we already fired BS triggers in this context */
2416	if (before_stmt_triggers_fired(RelationGetRelid(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc),
2417								   CMD_INSERT))
2418		return;
2419
2420	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2421	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT |
2422		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
2423	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2424	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2425	{
2426		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2427		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2428
2429		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2430								  TRIGGER_TYPE_STATEMENT,
2431								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
2432								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSERT))
2433			continue;
2434		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2435							NULL, NULL, NULL))
2436			continue;
2437
2438		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2439		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2440									   i,
2441									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2442									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2443									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2444
2445		if (newtuple)
2446			ereport(ERROR,
2447					(errcode(ERRCODE_E_R_I_E_TRIGGER_PROTOCOL_VIOLATED),
2448					 errmsg("BEFORE STATEMENT trigger cannot return a value")));
2449	}
2450}
2451
2452void
2453ExecASInsertTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2454					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture)
2455{
2456	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2457
2458	if (trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_insert_after_statement)
2459		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo, NULL, NULL,
2460							  TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT,
2461							  false, NULL, NULL, NIL, NULL, transition_capture,
2462							  false);
2463}
2464
2465bool
2466ExecBRInsertTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2467					 TupleTableSlot *slot)
2468{
2469	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2470	HeapTuple	newtuple = NULL;
2471	bool		should_free;
2472	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2473	int			i;
2474
2475	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2476	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT |
2477		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
2478		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
2479	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2480	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2481	{
2482		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2483		HeapTuple	oldtuple;
2484
2485		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2486								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
2487								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
2488								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSERT))
2489			continue;
2490		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2491							NULL, NULL, slot))
2492			continue;
2493
2494		if (!newtuple)
2495			newtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(slot, true, &should_free);
2496
2497		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = slot;
2498		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = oldtuple = newtuple;
2499		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2500		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2501									   i,
2502									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2503									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2504									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2505		if (newtuple == NULL)
2506		{
2507			if (should_free)
2508				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
2509			return false;		/* "do nothing" */
2510		}
2511		else if (newtuple != oldtuple)
2512		{
2513			newtuple = check_modified_virtual_generated(RelationGetDescr(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc), newtuple);
2514
2515			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(newtuple, slot, false);
2516
2517			/*
2518			 * After a tuple in a partition goes through a trigger, the user
2519			 * could have changed the partition key enough that the tuple no
2520			 * longer fits the partition.  Verify that.
2521			 */
2522			if (trigger->tgisclone &&
2523				!ExecPartitionCheck(relinfo, slot, estate, false))
2524				ereport(ERROR,
2525						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2526						 errmsg("moving row to another partition during a BEFORE FOR EACH ROW trigger is not supported"),
2527						 errdetail("Before executing trigger \"%s\", the row was to be in partition \"%s.%s\".",
2528								   trigger->tgname,
2529								   get_namespace_name(RelationGetNamespace(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc)),
2530								   RelationGetRelationName(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc))));
2531
2532			if (should_free)
2533				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
2534
2535			/* signal tuple should be re-fetched if used */
2536			newtuple = NULL;
2537		}
2538	}
2539
2540	return true;
2541}
2542
2543void
2544ExecARInsertTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2545					 TupleTableSlot *slot, List *recheckIndexes,
2546					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture)
2547{
2548	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2549
2550	if (relinfo->ri_FdwRoutine && transition_capture &&
2551		transition_capture->tcs_insert_new_table)
2552	{
2553		Assert(relinfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo);
2554		ereport(ERROR,
2555				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2556				 errmsg("cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables")));
2557	}
2558
2559	if ((trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_insert_after_row) ||
2560		(transition_capture && transition_capture->tcs_insert_new_table))
2561		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo, NULL, NULL,
2562							  TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT,
2563							  true, NULL, slot,
2564							  recheckIndexes, NULL,
2565							  transition_capture,
2566							  false);
2567}
2568
2569bool
2570ExecIRInsertTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2571					 TupleTableSlot *slot)
2572{
2573	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2574	HeapTuple	newtuple = NULL;
2575	bool		should_free;
2576	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2577	int			i;
2578
2579	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2580	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT |
2581		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
2582		TRIGGER_EVENT_INSTEAD;
2583	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2584	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2585	{
2586		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2587		HeapTuple	oldtuple;
2588
2589		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2590								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
2591								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSTEAD,
2592								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSERT))
2593			continue;
2594		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2595							NULL, NULL, slot))
2596			continue;
2597
2598		if (!newtuple)
2599			newtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(slot, true, &should_free);
2600
2601		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = slot;
2602		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = oldtuple = newtuple;
2603		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2604		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2605									   i,
2606									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2607									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2608									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2609		if (newtuple == NULL)
2610		{
2611			if (should_free)
2612				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
2613			return false;		/* "do nothing" */
2614		}
2615		else if (newtuple != oldtuple)
2616		{
2617			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(newtuple, slot, false);
2618
2619			if (should_free)
2620				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
2621
2622			/* signal tuple should be re-fetched if used */
2623			newtuple = NULL;
2624		}
2625	}
2626
2627	return true;
2628}
2629
2630void
2631ExecBSDeleteTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo)
2632{
2633	TriggerDesc *trigdesc;
2634	int			i;
2635	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2636
2637	trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2638
2639	if (trigdesc == NULL)
2640		return;
2641	if (!trigdesc->trig_delete_before_statement)
2642		return;
2643
2644	/* no-op if we already fired BS triggers in this context */
2645	if (before_stmt_triggers_fired(RelationGetRelid(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc),
2646								   CMD_DELETE))
2647		return;
2648
2649	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2650	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE |
2651		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
2652	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2653	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2654	{
2655		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2656		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2657
2658		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2659								  TRIGGER_TYPE_STATEMENT,
2660								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
2661								  TRIGGER_TYPE_DELETE))
2662			continue;
2663		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2664							NULL, NULL, NULL))
2665			continue;
2666
2667		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2668		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2669									   i,
2670									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2671									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2672									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2673
2674		if (newtuple)
2675			ereport(ERROR,
2676					(errcode(ERRCODE_E_R_I_E_TRIGGER_PROTOCOL_VIOLATED),
2677					 errmsg("BEFORE STATEMENT trigger cannot return a value")));
2678	}
2679}
2680
2681void
2682ExecASDeleteTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2683					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture)
2684{
2685	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2686
2687	if (trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_delete_after_statement)
2688		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo, NULL, NULL,
2689							  TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE,
2690							  false, NULL, NULL, NIL, NULL, transition_capture,
2691							  false);
2692}
2693
2694/*
2695 * Execute BEFORE ROW DELETE triggers.
2696 *
2697 * True indicates caller can proceed with the delete.  False indicates caller
2698 * need to suppress the delete and additionally if requested, we need to pass
2699 * back the concurrently updated tuple if any.
2700 */
2701bool
2702ExecBRDeleteTriggers(EState *estate, EPQState *epqstate,
2703					 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2704					 ItemPointer tupleid,
2705					 HeapTuple fdw_trigtuple,
2706					 TupleTableSlot **epqslot,
2707					 TM_Result *tmresult,
2708					 TM_FailureData *tmfd,
2709					 bool is_merge_delete)
2710{
2711	TupleTableSlot *slot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
2712	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2713	bool		result = true;
2714	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2715	HeapTuple	trigtuple;
2716	bool		should_free = false;
2717	int			i;
2718
2719	Assert(HeapTupleIsValid(fdw_trigtuple) ^ ItemPointerIsValid(tupleid));
2720	if (fdw_trigtuple == NULL)
2721	{
2722		TupleTableSlot *epqslot_candidate = NULL;
2723
2724		/*
2725		 * Get a copy of the on-disk tuple we are planning to delete.  In
2726		 * general, if the tuple has been concurrently updated, we should
2727		 * recheck it using EPQ.  However, if this is a MERGE DELETE action,
2728		 * we skip this EPQ recheck and leave it to the caller (it must do
2729		 * additional rechecking, and might end up executing a different
2730		 * action entirely).
2731		 */
2732		if (!GetTupleForTrigger(estate, epqstate, relinfo, tupleid,
2733								LockTupleExclusive, slot, !is_merge_delete,
2734								&epqslot_candidate, tmresult, tmfd))
2735			return false;
2736
2737		/*
2738		 * If the tuple was concurrently updated and the caller of this
2739		 * function requested for the updated tuple, skip the trigger
2740		 * execution.
2741		 */
2742		if (epqslot_candidate != NULL && epqslot != NULL)
2743		{
2744			*epqslot = epqslot_candidate;
2745			return false;
2746		}
2747
2748		trigtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(slot, true, &should_free);
2749	}
2750	else
2751	{
2752		trigtuple = fdw_trigtuple;
2753		ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(trigtuple, slot, false);
2754	}
2755
2756	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2757	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE |
2758		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
2759		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
2760	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2761	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2762	{
2763		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2764		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2765
2766		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2767								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
2768								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
2769								  TRIGGER_TYPE_DELETE))
2770			continue;
2771		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2772							NULL, slot, NULL))
2773			continue;
2774
2775		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = slot;
2776		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = trigtuple;
2777		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2778		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2779									   i,
2780									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2781									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2782									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2783		if (newtuple == NULL)
2784		{
2785			result = false;		/* tell caller to suppress delete */
2786			break;
2787		}
2788		if (newtuple != trigtuple)
2789			heap_freetuple(newtuple);
2790	}
2791	if (should_free)
2792		heap_freetuple(trigtuple);
2793
2794	return result;
2795}
2796
2797/*
2798 * Note: is_crosspart_update must be true if the DELETE is being performed
2799 * as part of a cross-partition update.
2800 */
2801void
2802ExecARDeleteTriggers(EState *estate,
2803					 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2804					 ItemPointer tupleid,
2805					 HeapTuple fdw_trigtuple,
2806					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture,
2807					 bool is_crosspart_update)
2808{
2809	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2810
2811	if (relinfo->ri_FdwRoutine && transition_capture &&
2812		transition_capture->tcs_delete_old_table)
2813	{
2814		Assert(relinfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo);
2815		ereport(ERROR,
2816				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2817				 errmsg("cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables")));
2818	}
2819
2820	if ((trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_delete_after_row) ||
2821		(transition_capture && transition_capture->tcs_delete_old_table))
2822	{
2823		TupleTableSlot *slot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
2824
2825		Assert(HeapTupleIsValid(fdw_trigtuple) ^ ItemPointerIsValid(tupleid));
2826		if (fdw_trigtuple == NULL)
2827			GetTupleForTrigger(estate,
2828							   NULL,
2829							   relinfo,
2830							   tupleid,
2831							   LockTupleExclusive,
2832							   slot,
2833							   false,
2834							   NULL,
2835							   NULL,
2836							   NULL);
2837		else
2838			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(fdw_trigtuple, slot, false);
2839
2840		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo, NULL, NULL,
2841							  TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE,
2842							  true, slot, NULL, NIL, NULL,
2843							  transition_capture,
2844							  is_crosspart_update);
2845	}
2846}
2847
2848bool
2849ExecIRDeleteTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2850					 HeapTuple trigtuple)
2851{
2852	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2853	TupleTableSlot *slot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
2854	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2855	int			i;
2856
2857	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2858	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE |
2859		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
2860		TRIGGER_EVENT_INSTEAD;
2861	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2862
2863	ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(trigtuple, slot, false);
2864
2865	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2866	{
2867		HeapTuple	rettuple;
2868		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2869
2870		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2871								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
2872								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSTEAD,
2873								  TRIGGER_TYPE_DELETE))
2874			continue;
2875		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2876							NULL, slot, NULL))
2877			continue;
2878
2879		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = slot;
2880		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = trigtuple;
2881		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2882		rettuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2883									   i,
2884									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2885									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2886									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2887		if (rettuple == NULL)
2888			return false;		/* Delete was suppressed */
2889		if (rettuple != trigtuple)
2890			heap_freetuple(rettuple);
2891	}
2892	return true;
2893}
2894
2895void
2896ExecBSUpdateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo)
2897{
2898	TriggerDesc *trigdesc;
2899	int			i;
2900	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2901	Bitmapset  *updatedCols;
2902
2903	trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2904
2905	if (trigdesc == NULL)
2906		return;
2907	if (!trigdesc->trig_update_before_statement)
2908		return;
2909
2910	/* no-op if we already fired BS triggers in this context */
2911	if (before_stmt_triggers_fired(RelationGetRelid(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc),
2912								   CMD_UPDATE))
2913		return;
2914
2915	/* statement-level triggers operate on the parent table */
2916	Assert(relinfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo == NULL);
2917
2918	updatedCols = ExecGetAllUpdatedCols(relinfo, estate);
2919
2920	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
2921	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE |
2922		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
2923	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
2924	LocTriggerData.tg_updatedcols = updatedCols;
2925	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
2926	{
2927		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
2928		HeapTuple	newtuple;
2929
2930		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
2931								  TRIGGER_TYPE_STATEMENT,
2932								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
2933								  TRIGGER_TYPE_UPDATE))
2934			continue;
2935		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
2936							updatedCols, NULL, NULL))
2937			continue;
2938
2939		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
2940		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
2941									   i,
2942									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
2943									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
2944									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
2945
2946		if (newtuple)
2947			ereport(ERROR,
2948					(errcode(ERRCODE_E_R_I_E_TRIGGER_PROTOCOL_VIOLATED),
2949					 errmsg("BEFORE STATEMENT trigger cannot return a value")));
2950	}
2951}
2952
2953void
2954ExecASUpdateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2955					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture)
2956{
2957	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2958
2959	/* statement-level triggers operate on the parent table */
2960	Assert(relinfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo == NULL);
2961
2962	if (trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_update_after_statement)
2963		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo, NULL, NULL,
2964							  TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE,
2965							  false, NULL, NULL, NIL,
2966							  ExecGetAllUpdatedCols(relinfo, estate),
2967							  transition_capture,
2968							  false);
2969}
2970
2971bool
2972ExecBRUpdateTriggers(EState *estate, EPQState *epqstate,
2973					 ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
2974					 ItemPointer tupleid,
2975					 HeapTuple fdw_trigtuple,
2976					 TupleTableSlot *newslot,
2977					 TM_Result *tmresult,
2978					 TM_FailureData *tmfd,
2979					 bool is_merge_update)
2980{
2981	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
2982	TupleTableSlot *oldslot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
2983	HeapTuple	newtuple = NULL;
2984	HeapTuple	trigtuple;
2985	bool		should_free_trig = false;
2986	bool		should_free_new = false;
2987	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
2988	int			i;
2989	Bitmapset  *updatedCols;
2990	LockTupleMode lockmode;
2991
2992	/* Determine lock mode to use */
2993	lockmode = ExecUpdateLockMode(estate, relinfo);
2994
2995	Assert(HeapTupleIsValid(fdw_trigtuple) ^ ItemPointerIsValid(tupleid));
2996	if (fdw_trigtuple == NULL)
2997	{
2998		TupleTableSlot *epqslot_candidate = NULL;
2999
3000		/*
3001		 * Get a copy of the on-disk tuple we are planning to update.  In
3002		 * general, if the tuple has been concurrently updated, we should
3003		 * recheck it using EPQ.  However, if this is a MERGE UPDATE action,
3004		 * we skip this EPQ recheck and leave it to the caller (it must do
3005		 * additional rechecking, and might end up executing a different
3006		 * action entirely).
3007		 */
3008		if (!GetTupleForTrigger(estate, epqstate, relinfo, tupleid,
3009								lockmode, oldslot, !is_merge_update,
3010								&epqslot_candidate, tmresult, tmfd))
3011			return false;		/* cancel the update action */
3012
3013		/*
3014		 * In READ COMMITTED isolation level it's possible that target tuple
3015		 * was changed due to concurrent update.  In that case we have a raw
3016		 * subplan output tuple in epqslot_candidate, and need to form a new
3017		 * insertable tuple using ExecGetUpdateNewTuple to replace the one we
3018		 * received in newslot.  Neither we nor our callers have any further
3019		 * interest in the passed-in tuple, so it's okay to overwrite newslot
3020		 * with the newer data.
3021		 */
3022		if (epqslot_candidate != NULL)
3023		{
3024			TupleTableSlot *epqslot_clean;
3025
3026			epqslot_clean = ExecGetUpdateNewTuple(relinfo, epqslot_candidate,
3027												  oldslot);
3028
3029			/*
3030			 * Typically, the caller's newslot was also generated by
3031			 * ExecGetUpdateNewTuple, so that epqslot_clean will be the same
3032			 * slot and copying is not needed.  But do the right thing if it
3033			 * isn't.
3034			 */
3035			if (unlikely(newslot != epqslot_clean))
3036				ExecCopySlot(newslot, epqslot_clean);
3037
3038			/*
3039			 * At this point newslot contains a virtual tuple that may
3040			 * reference some fields of oldslot's tuple in some disk buffer.
3041			 * If that tuple is in a different page than the original target
3042			 * tuple, then our only pin on that buffer is oldslot's, and we're
3043			 * about to release it.  Hence we'd better materialize newslot to
3044			 * ensure it doesn't contain references into an unpinned buffer.
3045			 * (We'd materialize it below anyway, but too late for safety.)
3046			 */
3047			ExecMaterializeSlot(newslot);
3048		}
3049
3050		/*
3051		 * Here we convert oldslot to a materialized slot holding trigtuple.
3052		 * Neither slot passed to the triggers will hold any buffer pin.
3053		 */
3054		trigtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(oldslot, true, &should_free_trig);
3055	}
3056	else
3057	{
3058		/* Put the FDW-supplied tuple into oldslot to unify the cases */
3059		ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(fdw_trigtuple, oldslot, false);
3060		trigtuple = fdw_trigtuple;
3061	}
3062
3063	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
3064	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE |
3065		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
3066		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
3067	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3068	updatedCols = ExecGetAllUpdatedCols(relinfo, estate);
3069	LocTriggerData.tg_updatedcols = updatedCols;
3070	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
3071	{
3072		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
3073		HeapTuple	oldtuple;
3074
3075		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
3076								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
3077								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
3078								  TRIGGER_TYPE_UPDATE))
3079			continue;
3080		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
3081							updatedCols, oldslot, newslot))
3082			continue;
3083
3084		if (!newtuple)
3085			newtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(newslot, true, &should_free_new);
3086
3087		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = oldslot;
3088		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = trigtuple;
3089		LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple = oldtuple = newtuple;
3090		LocTriggerData.tg_newslot = newslot;
3091		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
3092		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
3093									   i,
3094									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
3095									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
3096									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3097
3098		if (newtuple == NULL)
3099		{
3100			if (should_free_trig)
3101				heap_freetuple(trigtuple);
3102			if (should_free_new)
3103				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
3104			return false;		/* "do nothing" */
3105		}
3106		else if (newtuple != oldtuple)
3107		{
3108			newtuple = check_modified_virtual_generated(RelationGetDescr(relinfo->ri_RelationDesc), newtuple);
3109
3110			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(newtuple, newslot, false);
3111
3112			/*
3113			 * If the tuple returned by the trigger / being stored, is the old
3114			 * row version, and the heap tuple passed to the trigger was
3115			 * allocated locally, materialize the slot. Otherwise we might
3116			 * free it while still referenced by the slot.
3117			 */
3118			if (should_free_trig && newtuple == trigtuple)
3119				ExecMaterializeSlot(newslot);
3120
3121			if (should_free_new)
3122				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
3123
3124			/* signal tuple should be re-fetched if used */
3125			newtuple = NULL;
3126		}
3127	}
3128	if (should_free_trig)
3129		heap_freetuple(trigtuple);
3130
3131	return true;
3132}
3133
3134/*
3135 * Note: 'src_partinfo' and 'dst_partinfo', when non-NULL, refer to the source
3136 * and destination partitions, respectively, of a cross-partition update of
3137 * the root partitioned table mentioned in the query, given by 'relinfo'.
3138 * 'tupleid' in that case refers to the ctid of the "old" tuple in the source
3139 * partition, and 'newslot' contains the "new" tuple in the destination
3140 * partition.  This interface allows to support the requirements of
3141 * ExecCrossPartitionUpdateForeignKey(); is_crosspart_update must be true in
3142 * that case.
3143 */
3144void
3145ExecARUpdateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
3146					 ResultRelInfo *src_partinfo,
3147					 ResultRelInfo *dst_partinfo,
3148					 ItemPointer tupleid,
3149					 HeapTuple fdw_trigtuple,
3150					 TupleTableSlot *newslot,
3151					 List *recheckIndexes,
3152					 TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture,
3153					 bool is_crosspart_update)
3154{
3155	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
3156
3157	if (relinfo->ri_FdwRoutine && transition_capture &&
3158		(transition_capture->tcs_update_old_table ||
3159		 transition_capture->tcs_update_new_table))
3160	{
3161		Assert(relinfo->ri_RootResultRelInfo);
3162		ereport(ERROR,
3163				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3164				 errmsg("cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables")));
3165	}
3166
3167	if ((trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_update_after_row) ||
3168		(transition_capture &&
3169		 (transition_capture->tcs_update_old_table ||
3170		  transition_capture->tcs_update_new_table)))
3171	{
3172		/*
3173		 * Note: if the UPDATE is converted into a DELETE+INSERT as part of
3174		 * update-partition-key operation, then this function is also called
3175		 * separately for DELETE and INSERT to capture transition table rows.
3176		 * In such case, either old tuple or new tuple can be NULL.
3177		 */
3178		TupleTableSlot *oldslot;
3179		ResultRelInfo *tupsrc;
3180
3181		Assert((src_partinfo != NULL && dst_partinfo != NULL) ||
3182			   !is_crosspart_update);
3183
3184		tupsrc = src_partinfo ? src_partinfo : relinfo;
3185		oldslot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, tupsrc);
3186
3187		if (fdw_trigtuple == NULL && ItemPointerIsValid(tupleid))
3188			GetTupleForTrigger(estate,
3189							   NULL,
3190							   tupsrc,
3191							   tupleid,
3192							   LockTupleExclusive,
3193							   oldslot,
3194							   false,
3195							   NULL,
3196							   NULL,
3197							   NULL);
3198		else if (fdw_trigtuple != NULL)
3199			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(fdw_trigtuple, oldslot, false);
3200		else
3201			ExecClearTuple(oldslot);
3202
3203		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo,
3204							  src_partinfo, dst_partinfo,
3205							  TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE,
3206							  true,
3207							  oldslot, newslot, recheckIndexes,
3208							  ExecGetAllUpdatedCols(relinfo, estate),
3209							  transition_capture,
3210							  is_crosspart_update);
3211	}
3212}
3213
3214bool
3215ExecIRUpdateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
3216					 HeapTuple trigtuple, TupleTableSlot *newslot)
3217{
3218	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
3219	TupleTableSlot *oldslot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
3220	HeapTuple	newtuple = NULL;
3221	bool		should_free;
3222	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
3223	int			i;
3224
3225	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
3226	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE |
3227		TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW |
3228		TRIGGER_EVENT_INSTEAD;
3229	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3230
3231	ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(trigtuple, oldslot, false);
3232
3233	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
3234	{
3235		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
3236		HeapTuple	oldtuple;
3237
3238		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
3239								  TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW,
3240								  TRIGGER_TYPE_INSTEAD,
3241								  TRIGGER_TYPE_UPDATE))
3242			continue;
3243		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
3244							NULL, oldslot, newslot))
3245			continue;
3246
3247		if (!newtuple)
3248			newtuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(newslot, true, &should_free);
3249
3250		LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = oldslot;
3251		LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = trigtuple;
3252		LocTriggerData.tg_newslot = newslot;
3253		LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple = oldtuple = newtuple;
3254
3255		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
3256		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
3257									   i,
3258									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
3259									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
3260									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3261		if (newtuple == NULL)
3262		{
3263			return false;		/* "do nothing" */
3264		}
3265		else if (newtuple != oldtuple)
3266		{
3267			ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(newtuple, newslot, false);
3268
3269			if (should_free)
3270				heap_freetuple(oldtuple);
3271
3272			/* signal tuple should be re-fetched if used */
3273			newtuple = NULL;
3274		}
3275	}
3276
3277	return true;
3278}
3279
3280void
3281ExecBSTruncateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo)
3282{
3283	TriggerDesc *trigdesc;
3284	int			i;
3285	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
3286
3287	trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
3288
3289	if (trigdesc == NULL)
3290		return;
3291	if (!trigdesc->trig_truncate_before_statement)
3292		return;
3293
3294	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
3295	LocTriggerData.tg_event = TRIGGER_EVENT_TRUNCATE |
3296		TRIGGER_EVENT_BEFORE;
3297	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3298
3299	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
3300	{
3301		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
3302		HeapTuple	newtuple;
3303
3304		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
3305								  TRIGGER_TYPE_STATEMENT,
3306								  TRIGGER_TYPE_BEFORE,
3307								  TRIGGER_TYPE_TRUNCATE))
3308			continue;
3309		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, LocTriggerData.tg_event,
3310							NULL, NULL, NULL))
3311			continue;
3312
3313		LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = trigger;
3314		newtuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
3315									   i,
3316									   relinfo->ri_TrigFunctions,
3317									   relinfo->ri_TrigInstrument,
3318									   GetPerTupleMemoryContext(estate));
3319
3320		if (newtuple)
3321			ereport(ERROR,
3322					(errcode(ERRCODE_E_R_I_E_TRIGGER_PROTOCOL_VIOLATED),
3323					 errmsg("BEFORE STATEMENT trigger cannot return a value")));
3324	}
3325}
3326
3327void
3328ExecASTruncateTriggers(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo)
3329{
3330	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
3331
3332	if (trigdesc && trigdesc->trig_truncate_after_statement)
3333		AfterTriggerSaveEvent(estate, relinfo,
3334							  NULL, NULL,
3335							  TRIGGER_EVENT_TRUNCATE,
3336							  false, NULL, NULL, NIL, NULL, NULL,
3337							  false);
3338}
3339
3340
3341/*
3342 * Fetch tuple into "oldslot", dealing with locking and EPQ if necessary
3343 */
3344static bool
3345GetTupleForTrigger(EState *estate,
3346				   EPQState *epqstate,
3347				   ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
3348				   ItemPointer tid,
3349				   LockTupleMode lockmode,
3350				   TupleTableSlot *oldslot,
3351				   bool do_epq_recheck,
3352				   TupleTableSlot **epqslot,
3353				   TM_Result *tmresultp,
3354				   TM_FailureData *tmfdp)
3355{
3356	Relation	relation = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
3357
3358	if (epqslot != NULL)
3359	{
3360		TM_Result	test;
3361		TM_FailureData tmfd;
3362		int			lockflags = 0;
3363
3364		*epqslot = NULL;
3365
3366		/* caller must pass an epqstate if EvalPlanQual is possible */
3367		Assert(epqstate != NULL);
3368
3369		/*
3370		 * lock tuple for update
3371		 */
3372		if (!IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
3373			lockflags |= TUPLE_LOCK_FLAG_FIND_LAST_VERSION;
3374		test = table_tuple_lock(relation, tid, estate->es_snapshot, oldslot,
3375								estate->es_output_cid,
3376								lockmode, LockWaitBlock,
3377								lockflags,
3378								&tmfd);
3379
3380		/* Let the caller know about the status of this operation */
3381		if (tmresultp)
3382			*tmresultp = test;
3383		if (tmfdp)
3384			*tmfdp = tmfd;
3385
3386		switch (test)
3387		{
3388			case TM_SelfModified:
3389
3390				/*
3391				 * The target tuple was already updated or deleted by the
3392				 * current command, or by a later command in the current
3393				 * transaction.  We ignore the tuple in the former case, and
3394				 * throw error in the latter case, for the same reasons
3395				 * enumerated in ExecUpdate and ExecDelete in
3396				 * nodeModifyTable.c.
3397				 */
3398				if (tmfd.cmax != estate->es_output_cid)
3399					ereport(ERROR,
3400							(errcode(ERRCODE_TRIGGERED_DATA_CHANGE_VIOLATION),
3401							 errmsg("tuple to be updated was already modified by an operation triggered by the current command"),
3402							 errhint("Consider using an AFTER trigger instead of a BEFORE trigger to propagate changes to other rows.")));
3403
3404				/* treat it as deleted; do not process */
3405				return false;
3406
3407			case TM_Ok:
3408				if (tmfd.traversed)
3409				{
3410					/*
3411					 * Recheck the tuple using EPQ, if requested.  Otherwise,
3412					 * just return that it was concurrently updated.
3413					 */
3414					if (do_epq_recheck)
3415					{
3416						*epqslot = EvalPlanQual(epqstate,
3417												relation,
3418												relinfo->ri_RangeTableIndex,
3419												oldslot);
3420
3421						/*
3422						 * If PlanQual failed for updated tuple - we must not
3423						 * process this tuple!
3424						 */
3425						if (TupIsNull(*epqslot))
3426						{
3427							*epqslot = NULL;
3428							return false;
3429						}
3430					}
3431					else
3432					{
3433						if (tmresultp)
3434							*tmresultp = TM_Updated;
3435						return false;
3436					}
3437				}
3438				break;
3439
3440			case TM_Updated:
3441				if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
3442					ereport(ERROR,
3443							(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
3444							 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent update")));
3445				elog(ERROR, "unexpected table_tuple_lock status: %u", test);
3446				break;
3447
3448			case TM_Deleted:
3449				if (IsolationUsesXactSnapshot())
3450					ereport(ERROR,
3451							(errcode(ERRCODE_T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE),
3452							 errmsg("could not serialize access due to concurrent delete")));
3453				/* tuple was deleted */
3454				return false;
3455
3456			case TM_Invisible:
3457				elog(ERROR, "attempted to lock invisible tuple");
3458				break;
3459
3460			default:
3461				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized table_tuple_lock status: %u", test);
3462				return false;	/* keep compiler quiet */
3463		}
3464	}
3465	else
3466	{
3467		/*
3468		 * We expect the tuple to be present, thus very simple error handling
3469		 * suffices.
3470		 */
3471		if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(relation, tid, SnapshotAny,
3472										   oldslot))
3473			elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple for trigger");
3474	}
3475
3476	return true;
3477}
3478
3479/*
3480 * Is trigger enabled to fire?
3481 */
3482static bool
3483TriggerEnabled(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
3484			   Trigger *trigger, TriggerEvent event,
3485			   Bitmapset *modifiedCols,
3486			   TupleTableSlot *oldslot, TupleTableSlot *newslot)
3487{
3488	/* Check replication-role-dependent enable state */
3489	if (SessionReplicationRole == SESSION_REPLICATION_ROLE_REPLICA)
3490	{
3491		if (trigger->tgenabled == TRIGGER_FIRES_ON_ORIGIN ||
3492			trigger->tgenabled == TRIGGER_DISABLED)
3493			return false;
3494	}
3495	else						/* ORIGIN or LOCAL role */
3496	{
3497		if (trigger->tgenabled == TRIGGER_FIRES_ON_REPLICA ||
3498			trigger->tgenabled == TRIGGER_DISABLED)
3499			return false;
3500	}
3501
3502	/*
3503	 * Check for column-specific trigger (only possible for UPDATE, and in
3504	 * fact we *must* ignore tgattr for other event types)
3505	 */
3506	if (trigger->tgnattr > 0 && TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_UPDATE(event))
3507	{
3508		int			i;
3509		bool		modified;
3510
3511		modified = false;
3512		for (i = 0; i < trigger->tgnattr; i++)
3513		{
3514			if (bms_is_member(trigger->tgattr[i] - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,
3515							  modifiedCols))
3516			{
3517				modified = true;
3518				break;
3519			}
3520		}
3521		if (!modified)
3522			return false;
3523	}
3524
3525	/* Check for WHEN clause */
3526	if (trigger->tgqual)
3527	{
3528		ExprState **predicate;
3529		ExprContext *econtext;
3530		MemoryContext oldContext;
3531		int			i;
3532
3533		Assert(estate != NULL);
3534
3535		/*
3536		 * trigger is an element of relinfo->ri_TrigDesc->triggers[]; find the
3537		 * matching element of relinfo->ri_TrigWhenExprs[]
3538		 */
3539		i = trigger - relinfo->ri_TrigDesc->triggers;
3540		predicate = &relinfo->ri_TrigWhenExprs[i];
3541
3542		/*
3543		 * If first time through for this WHEN expression, build expression
3544		 * nodetrees for it.  Keep them in the per-query memory context so
3545		 * they'll survive throughout the query.
3546		 */
3547		if (*predicate == NULL)
3548		{
3549			Node	   *tgqual;
3550
3551			oldContext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(estate->es_query_cxt);
3552			tgqual = stringToNode(trigger->tgqual);
3553			tgqual = expand_generated_columns_in_expr(tgqual, relinfo->ri_RelationDesc, PRS2_OLD_VARNO);
3554			tgqual = expand_generated_columns_in_expr(tgqual, relinfo->ri_RelationDesc, PRS2_NEW_VARNO);
3555			/* Change references to OLD and NEW to INNER_VAR and OUTER_VAR */
3556			ChangeVarNodes(tgqual, PRS2_OLD_VARNO, INNER_VAR, 0);
3557			ChangeVarNodes(tgqual, PRS2_NEW_VARNO, OUTER_VAR, 0);
3558			/* ExecPrepareQual wants implicit-AND form */
3559			tgqual = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) tgqual);
3560			*predicate = ExecPrepareQual((List *) tgqual, estate);
3561			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldContext);
3562		}
3563
3564		/*
3565		 * We will use the EState's per-tuple context for evaluating WHEN
3566		 * expressions (creating it if it's not already there).
3567		 */
3568		econtext = GetPerTupleExprContext(estate);
3569
3570		/*
3571		 * Finally evaluate the expression, making the old and/or new tuples
3572		 * available as INNER_VAR/OUTER_VAR respectively.
3573		 */
3574		econtext->ecxt_innertuple = oldslot;
3575		econtext->ecxt_outertuple = newslot;
3576		if (!ExecQual(*predicate, econtext))
3577			return false;
3578	}
3579
3580	return true;
3581}
3582
3583
3584/* ----------
3585 * After-trigger stuff
3586 *
3587 * The AfterTriggersData struct holds data about pending AFTER trigger events
3588 * during the current transaction tree.  (BEFORE triggers are fired
3589 * immediately so we don't need any persistent state about them.)  The struct
3590 * and most of its subsidiary data are kept in TopTransactionContext; however
3591 * some data that can be discarded sooner appears in the CurTransactionContext
3592 * of the relevant subtransaction.  Also, the individual event records are
3593 * kept in a separate sub-context of TopTransactionContext.  This is done
3594 * mainly so that it's easy to tell from a memory context dump how much space
3595 * is being eaten by trigger events.
3596 *
3597 * Because the list of pending events can grow large, we go to some
3598 * considerable effort to minimize per-event memory consumption.  The event
3599 * records are grouped into chunks and common data for similar events in the
3600 * same chunk is only stored once.
3601 *
3602 * XXX We need to be able to save the per-event data in a file if it grows too
3603 * large.
3604 * ----------
3605 */
3606
3607/* Per-trigger SET CONSTRAINT status */
3608typedef struct SetConstraintTriggerData
3609{
3610	Oid			sct_tgoid;
3611	bool		sct_tgisdeferred;
3612} SetConstraintTriggerData;
3613
3614typedef struct SetConstraintTriggerData *SetConstraintTrigger;
3615
3616/*
3617 * SET CONSTRAINT intra-transaction status.
3618 *
3619 * We make this a single palloc'd object so it can be copied and freed easily.
3620 *
3621 * all_isset and all_isdeferred are used to keep track
3622 * of SET CONSTRAINTS ALL {DEFERRED, IMMEDIATE}.
3623 *
3624 * trigstates[] stores per-trigger tgisdeferred settings.
3625 */
3626typedef struct SetConstraintStateData
3627{
3628	bool		all_isset;
3629	bool		all_isdeferred;
3630	int			numstates;		/* number of trigstates[] entries in use */
3631	int			numalloc;		/* allocated size of trigstates[] */
3632	SetConstraintTriggerData trigstates[FLEXIBLE_ARRAY_MEMBER];
3633} SetConstraintStateData;
3634
3635typedef SetConstraintStateData *SetConstraintState;
3636
3637
3638/*
3639 * Per-trigger-event data
3640 *
3641 * The actual per-event data, AfterTriggerEventData, includes DONE/IN_PROGRESS
3642 * status bits, up to two tuple CTIDs, and optionally two OIDs of partitions.
3643 * Each event record also has an associated AfterTriggerSharedData that is
3644 * shared across all instances of similar events within a "chunk".
3645 *
3646 * For row-level triggers, we arrange not to waste storage on unneeded ctid
3647 * fields.  Updates of regular tables use two; inserts and deletes of regular
3648 * tables use one; foreign tables always use zero and save the tuple(s) to a
3649 * tuplestore.  AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_FETCH directs AfterTriggerExecute() to
3650 * retrieve a fresh tuple or pair of tuples from that tuplestore, while
3651 * AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_REUSE directs it to use the most-recently-retrieved
3652 * tuple(s).  This permits storing tuples once regardless of the number of
3653 * row-level triggers on a foreign table.
3654 *
3655 * When updates on partitioned tables cause rows to move between partitions,
3656 * the OIDs of both partitions are stored too, so that the tuples can be
3657 * fetched; such entries are marked AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE (for "cross-
3658 * partition update").
3659 *
3660 * Note that we need triggers on foreign tables to be fired in exactly the
3661 * order they were queued, so that the tuples come out of the tuplestore in
3662 * the right order.  To ensure that, we forbid deferrable (constraint)
3663 * triggers on foreign tables.  This also ensures that such triggers do not
3664 * get deferred into outer trigger query levels, meaning that it's okay to
3665 * destroy the tuplestore at the end of the query level.
3666 *
3667 * Statement-level triggers always bear AFTER_TRIGGER_1CTID, though they
3668 * require no ctid field.  We lack the flag bit space to neatly represent that
3669 * distinct case, and it seems unlikely to be worth much trouble.
3670 *
3671 * Note: ats_firing_id is initially zero and is set to something else when
3672 * AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS is set.  It indicates which trigger firing
3673 * cycle the trigger will be fired in (or was fired in, if DONE is set).
3674 * Although this is mutable state, we can keep it in AfterTriggerSharedData
3675 * because all instances of the same type of event in a given event list will
3676 * be fired at the same time, if they were queued between the same firing
3677 * cycles.  So we need only ensure that ats_firing_id is zero when attaching
3678 * a new event to an existing AfterTriggerSharedData record.
3679 */
3680typedef uint32 TriggerFlags;
3681
3682#define AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET			0x07FFFFFF	/* must be low-order bits */
3683#define AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE				0x80000000
3684#define AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS		0x40000000
3685/* bits describing the size and tuple sources of this event */
3686#define AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_REUSE			0x00000000
3687#define AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_FETCH			0x20000000
3688#define AFTER_TRIGGER_1CTID				0x10000000
3689#define AFTER_TRIGGER_2CTID				0x30000000
3690#define AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE			0x08000000
3691#define AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS			0x38000000
3692typedef struct AfterTriggerSharedData *AfterTriggerShared;
3693
3694typedef struct AfterTriggerSharedData
3695{
3696	TriggerEvent ats_event;		/* event type indicator, see trigger.h */
3697	Oid			ats_tgoid;		/* the trigger's ID */
3698	Oid			ats_relid;		/* the relation it's on */
3699	Oid			ats_rolid;		/* role to execute the trigger */
3700	CommandId	ats_firing_id;	/* ID for firing cycle */
3701	struct AfterTriggersTableData *ats_table;	/* transition table access */
3702	Bitmapset  *ats_modifiedcols;	/* modified columns */
3703} AfterTriggerSharedData;
3704
3705typedef struct AfterTriggerEventData *AfterTriggerEvent;
3706
3707typedef struct AfterTriggerEventData
3708{
3709	TriggerFlags ate_flags;		/* status bits and offset to shared data */
3710	ItemPointerData ate_ctid1;	/* inserted, deleted, or old updated tuple */
3711	ItemPointerData ate_ctid2;	/* new updated tuple */
3712
3713	/*
3714	 * During a cross-partition update of a partitioned table, we also store
3715	 * the OIDs of source and destination partitions that are needed to fetch
3716	 * the old (ctid1) and the new tuple (ctid2) from, respectively.
3717	 */
3718	Oid			ate_src_part;
3719	Oid			ate_dst_part;
3720} AfterTriggerEventData;
3721
3722/* AfterTriggerEventData, minus ate_src_part, ate_dst_part */
3723typedef struct AfterTriggerEventDataNoOids
3724{
3725	TriggerFlags ate_flags;
3726	ItemPointerData ate_ctid1;
3727	ItemPointerData ate_ctid2;
3728}			AfterTriggerEventDataNoOids;
3729
3730/* AfterTriggerEventData, minus ate_*_part and ate_ctid2 */
3731typedef struct AfterTriggerEventDataOneCtid
3732{
3733	TriggerFlags ate_flags;		/* status bits and offset to shared data */
3734	ItemPointerData ate_ctid1;	/* inserted, deleted, or old updated tuple */
3735}			AfterTriggerEventDataOneCtid;
3736
3737/* AfterTriggerEventData, minus ate_*_part, ate_ctid1 and ate_ctid2 */
3738typedef struct AfterTriggerEventDataZeroCtids
3739{
3740	TriggerFlags ate_flags;		/* status bits and offset to shared data */
3741}			AfterTriggerEventDataZeroCtids;
3742
3743#define SizeofTriggerEvent(evt) \
3744	(((evt)->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS) == AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE ? \
3745	 sizeof(AfterTriggerEventData) : \
3746	 (((evt)->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS) == AFTER_TRIGGER_2CTID ? \
3747	  sizeof(AfterTriggerEventDataNoOids) : \
3748	  (((evt)->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS) == AFTER_TRIGGER_1CTID ? \
3749	   sizeof(AfterTriggerEventDataOneCtid) : \
3750	   sizeof(AfterTriggerEventDataZeroCtids))))
3751
3752#define GetTriggerSharedData(evt) \
3753	((AfterTriggerShared) ((char *) (evt) + ((evt)->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET)))
3754
3755/*
3756 * To avoid palloc overhead, we keep trigger events in arrays in successively-
3757 * larger chunks (a slightly more sophisticated version of an expansible
3758 * array).  The space between CHUNK_DATA_START and freeptr is occupied by
3759 * AfterTriggerEventData records; the space between endfree and endptr is
3760 * occupied by AfterTriggerSharedData records.
3761 */
3762typedef struct AfterTriggerEventChunk
3763{
3764	struct AfterTriggerEventChunk *next;	/* list link */
3765	char	   *freeptr;		/* start of free space in chunk */
3766	char	   *endfree;		/* end of free space in chunk */
3767	char	   *endptr;			/* end of chunk */
3768	/* event data follows here */
3769} AfterTriggerEventChunk;
3770
3771#define CHUNK_DATA_START(cptr) ((char *) (cptr) + MAXALIGN(sizeof(AfterTriggerEventChunk)))
3772
3773/* A list of events */
3774typedef struct AfterTriggerEventList
3775{
3776	AfterTriggerEventChunk *head;
3777	AfterTriggerEventChunk *tail;
3778	char	   *tailfree;		/* freeptr of tail chunk */
3779} AfterTriggerEventList;
3780
3781/* Macros to help in iterating over a list of events */
3782#define for_each_chunk(cptr, evtlist) \
3783	for (cptr = (evtlist).head; cptr != NULL; cptr = cptr->next)
3784#define for_each_event(eptr, cptr) \
3785	for (eptr = (AfterTriggerEvent) CHUNK_DATA_START(cptr); \
3786		 (char *) eptr < (cptr)->freeptr; \
3787		 eptr = (AfterTriggerEvent) (((char *) eptr) + SizeofTriggerEvent(eptr)))
3788/* Use this if no special per-chunk processing is needed */
3789#define for_each_event_chunk(eptr, cptr, evtlist) \
3790	for_each_chunk(cptr, evtlist) for_each_event(eptr, cptr)
3791
3792/* Macros for iterating from a start point that might not be list start */
3793#define for_each_chunk_from(cptr) \
3794	for (; cptr != NULL; cptr = cptr->next)
3795#define for_each_event_from(eptr, cptr) \
3796	for (; \
3797		 (char *) eptr < (cptr)->freeptr; \
3798		 eptr = (AfterTriggerEvent) (((char *) eptr) + SizeofTriggerEvent(eptr)))
3799
3800
3801/*
3802 * All per-transaction data for the AFTER TRIGGERS module.
3803 *
3804 * AfterTriggersData has the following fields:
3805 *
3806 * firing_counter is incremented for each call of afterTriggerInvokeEvents.
3807 * We mark firable events with the current firing cycle's ID so that we can
3808 * tell which ones to work on.  This ensures sane behavior if a trigger
3809 * function chooses to do SET CONSTRAINTS: the inner SET CONSTRAINTS will
3810 * only fire those events that weren't already scheduled for firing.
3811 *
3812 * state keeps track of the transaction-local effects of SET CONSTRAINTS.
3813 * This is saved and restored across failed subtransactions.
3814 *
3815 * events is the current list of deferred events.  This is global across
3816 * all subtransactions of the current transaction.  In a subtransaction
3817 * abort, we know that the events added by the subtransaction are at the
3818 * end of the list, so it is relatively easy to discard them.  The event
3819 * list chunks themselves are stored in event_cxt.
3820 *
3821 * query_depth is the current depth of nested AfterTriggerBeginQuery calls
3822 * (-1 when the stack is empty).
3823 *
3824 * query_stack[query_depth] is the per-query-level data, including these fields:
3825 *
3826 * events is a list of AFTER trigger events queued by the current query.
3827 * None of these are valid until the matching AfterTriggerEndQuery call
3828 * occurs.  At that point we fire immediate-mode triggers, and append any
3829 * deferred events to the main events list.
3830 *
3831 * fdw_tuplestore is a tuplestore containing the foreign-table tuples
3832 * needed by events queued by the current query.  (Note: we use just one
3833 * tuplestore even though more than one foreign table might be involved.
3834 * This is okay because tuplestores don't really care what's in the tuples
3835 * they store; but it's possible that someday it'd break.)
3836 *
3837 * tables is a List of AfterTriggersTableData structs for target tables
3838 * of the current query (see below).
3839 *
3840 * maxquerydepth is just the allocated length of query_stack.
3841 *
3842 * trans_stack holds per-subtransaction data, including these fields:
3843 *
3844 * state is NULL or a pointer to a saved copy of the SET CONSTRAINTS
3845 * state data.  Each subtransaction level that modifies that state first
3846 * saves a copy, which we use to restore the state if we abort.
3847 *
3848 * events is a copy of the events head/tail pointers,
3849 * which we use to restore those values during subtransaction abort.
3850 *
3851 * query_depth is the subtransaction-start-time value of query_depth,
3852 * which we similarly use to clean up at subtransaction abort.
3853 *
3854 * firing_counter is the subtransaction-start-time value of firing_counter.
3855 * We use this to recognize which deferred triggers were fired (or marked
3856 * for firing) within an aborted subtransaction.
3857 *
3858 * We use GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel() to determine the correct array
3859 * index in trans_stack.  maxtransdepth is the number of allocated entries in
3860 * trans_stack.  (By not keeping our own stack pointer, we can avoid trouble
3861 * in cases where errors during subxact abort cause multiple invocations
3862 * of AfterTriggerEndSubXact() at the same nesting depth.)
3863 *
3864 * We create an AfterTriggersTableData struct for each target table of the
3865 * current query, and each operation mode (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), that has
3866 * either transition tables or statement-level triggers.  This is used to
3867 * hold the relevant transition tables, as well as info tracking whether
3868 * we already queued the statement triggers.  (We use that info to prevent
3869 * firing the same statement triggers more than once per statement, or really
3870 * once per transition table set.)  These structs, along with the transition
3871 * table tuplestores, live in the (sub)transaction's CurTransactionContext.
3872 * That's sufficient lifespan because we don't allow transition tables to be
3873 * used by deferrable triggers, so they only need to survive until
3874 * AfterTriggerEndQuery.
3875 */
3876typedef struct AfterTriggersQueryData AfterTriggersQueryData;
3877typedef struct AfterTriggersTransData AfterTriggersTransData;
3878typedef struct AfterTriggersTableData AfterTriggersTableData;
3879
3880typedef struct AfterTriggersData
3881{
3882	CommandId	firing_counter; /* next firing ID to assign */
3883	SetConstraintState state;	/* the active S C state */
3884	AfterTriggerEventList events;	/* deferred-event list */
3885	MemoryContext event_cxt;	/* memory context for events, if any */
3886
3887	/* per-query-level data: */
3888	AfterTriggersQueryData *query_stack;	/* array of structs shown below */
3889	int			query_depth;	/* current index in above array */
3890	int			maxquerydepth;	/* allocated len of above array */
3891
3892	/* per-subtransaction-level data: */
3893	AfterTriggersTransData *trans_stack;	/* array of structs shown below */
3894	int			maxtransdepth;	/* allocated len of above array */
3895} AfterTriggersData;
3896
3897struct AfterTriggersQueryData
3898{
3899	AfterTriggerEventList events;	/* events pending from this query */
3900	Tuplestorestate *fdw_tuplestore;	/* foreign tuples for said events */
3901	List	   *tables;			/* list of AfterTriggersTableData, see below */
3902};
3903
3904struct AfterTriggersTransData
3905{
3906	/* these fields are just for resetting at subtrans abort: */
3907	SetConstraintState state;	/* saved S C state, or NULL if not yet saved */
3908	AfterTriggerEventList events;	/* saved list pointer */
3909	int			query_depth;	/* saved query_depth */
3910	CommandId	firing_counter; /* saved firing_counter */
3911};
3912
3913struct AfterTriggersTableData
3914{
3915	/* relid + cmdType form the lookup key for these structs: */
3916	Oid			relid;			/* target table's OID */
3917	CmdType		cmdType;		/* event type, CMD_INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE */
3918	bool		closed;			/* true when no longer OK to add tuples */
3919	bool		before_trig_done;	/* did we already queue BS triggers? */
3920	bool		after_trig_done;	/* did we already queue AS triggers? */
3921	AfterTriggerEventList after_trig_events;	/* if so, saved list pointer */
3922
3923	/* "old" transition table for UPDATE/DELETE, if any */
3924	Tuplestorestate *old_tuplestore;
3925	/* "new" transition table for INSERT/UPDATE, if any */
3926	Tuplestorestate *new_tuplestore;
3927
3928	TupleTableSlot *storeslot;	/* for converting to tuplestore's format */
3929};
3930
3931static AfterTriggersData afterTriggers;
3932
3933static void AfterTriggerExecute(EState *estate,
3934								AfterTriggerEvent event,
3935								ResultRelInfo *relInfo,
3936								ResultRelInfo *src_relInfo,
3937								ResultRelInfo *dst_relInfo,
3938								TriggerDesc *trigdesc,
3939								FmgrInfo *finfo,
3940								Instrumentation *instr,
3941								MemoryContext per_tuple_context,
3942								TupleTableSlot *trig_tuple_slot1,
3943								TupleTableSlot *trig_tuple_slot2);
3944static AfterTriggersTableData *GetAfterTriggersTableData(Oid relid,
3945														 CmdType cmdType);
3946static TupleTableSlot *GetAfterTriggersStoreSlot(AfterTriggersTableData *table,
3947												 TupleDesc tupdesc);
3948static Tuplestorestate *GetAfterTriggersTransitionTable(int event,
3949														TupleTableSlot *oldslot,
3950														TupleTableSlot *newslot,
3951														TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture);
3952static void TransitionTableAddTuple(EState *estate,
3953									int event,
3954									TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture,
3955									ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
3956									TupleTableSlot *slot,
3957									TupleTableSlot *original_insert_tuple,
3958									Tuplestorestate *tuplestore);
3959static void AfterTriggerFreeQuery(AfterTriggersQueryData *qs);
3960static SetConstraintState SetConstraintStateCreate(int numalloc);
3961static SetConstraintState SetConstraintStateCopy(SetConstraintState origstate);
3962static SetConstraintState SetConstraintStateAddItem(SetConstraintState state,
3963													Oid tgoid, bool tgisdeferred);
3964static void cancel_prior_stmt_triggers(Oid relid, CmdType cmdType, int tgevent);
3965
3966
3967/*
3968 * Get the FDW tuplestore for the current trigger query level, creating it
3969 * if necessary.
3970 */
3971static Tuplestorestate *
3972GetCurrentFDWTuplestore(void)
3973{
3974	Tuplestorestate *ret;
3975
3976	ret = afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth].fdw_tuplestore;
3977	if (ret == NULL)
3978	{
3979		MemoryContext oldcxt;
3980		ResourceOwner saveResourceOwner;
3981
3982		/*
3983		 * Make the tuplestore valid until end of subtransaction.  We really
3984		 * only need it until AfterTriggerEndQuery().
3985		 */
3986		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CurTransactionContext);
3987		saveResourceOwner = CurrentResourceOwner;
3988		CurrentResourceOwner = CurTransactionResourceOwner;
3989
3990		ret = tuplestore_begin_heap(false, false, work_mem);
3991
3992		CurrentResourceOwner = saveResourceOwner;
3993		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
3994
3995		afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth].fdw_tuplestore = ret;
3996	}
3997
3998	return ret;
3999}
4000
4001/* ----------
4002 * afterTriggerCheckState()
4003 *
4004 *	Returns true if the trigger event is actually in state DEFERRED.
4005 * ----------
4006 */
4007static bool
4008afterTriggerCheckState(AfterTriggerShared evtshared)
4009{
4010	Oid			tgoid = evtshared->ats_tgoid;
4011	SetConstraintState state = afterTriggers.state;
4012	int			i;
4013
4014	/*
4015	 * For not-deferrable triggers (i.e. normal AFTER ROW triggers and
4016	 * constraints declared NOT DEFERRABLE), the state is always false.
4017	 */
4018	if ((evtshared->ats_event & AFTER_TRIGGER_DEFERRABLE) == 0)
4019		return false;
4020
4021	/*
4022	 * If constraint state exists, SET CONSTRAINTS might have been executed
4023	 * either for this trigger or for all triggers.
4024	 */
4025	if (state != NULL)
4026	{
4027		/* Check for SET CONSTRAINTS for this specific trigger. */
4028		for (i = 0; i < state->numstates; i++)
4029		{
4030			if (state->trigstates[i].sct_tgoid == tgoid)
4031				return state->trigstates[i].sct_tgisdeferred;
4032		}
4033
4034		/* Check for SET CONSTRAINTS ALL. */
4035		if (state->all_isset)
4036			return state->all_isdeferred;
4037	}
4038
4039	/*
4040	 * Otherwise return the default state for the trigger.
4041	 */
4042	return ((evtshared->ats_event & AFTER_TRIGGER_INITDEFERRED) != 0);
4043}
4044
4045/* ----------
4046 * afterTriggerCopyBitmap()
4047 *
4048 * Copy bitmap into AfterTriggerEvents memory context, which is where the after
4049 * trigger events are kept.
4050 * ----------
4051 */
4052static Bitmapset *
4053afterTriggerCopyBitmap(Bitmapset *src)
4054{
4055	Bitmapset  *dst;
4056	MemoryContext oldcxt;
4057
4058	if (src == NULL)
4059		return NULL;
4060
4061	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(afterTriggers.event_cxt);
4062
4063	dst = bms_copy(src);
4064
4065	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4066
4067	return dst;
4068}
4069
4070/* ----------
4071 * afterTriggerAddEvent()
4072 *
4073 *	Add a new trigger event to the specified queue.
4074 *	The passed-in event data is copied.
4075 * ----------
4076 */
4077static void
4078afterTriggerAddEvent(AfterTriggerEventList *events,
4079					 AfterTriggerEvent event, AfterTriggerShared evtshared)
4080{
4081	Size		eventsize = SizeofTriggerEvent(event);
4082	Size		needed = eventsize + sizeof(AfterTriggerSharedData);
4083	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
4084	AfterTriggerShared newshared;
4085	AfterTriggerEvent newevent;
4086
4087	/*
4088	 * If empty list or not enough room in the tail chunk, make a new chunk.
4089	 * We assume here that a new shared record will always be needed.
4090	 */
4091	chunk = events->tail;
4092	if (chunk == NULL ||
4093		chunk->endfree - chunk->freeptr < needed)
4094	{
4095		Size		chunksize;
4096
4097		/* Create event context if we didn't already */
4098		if (afterTriggers.event_cxt == NULL)
4099			afterTriggers.event_cxt =
4100				AllocSetContextCreate(TopTransactionContext,
4101									  "AfterTriggerEvents",
4102									  ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
4103
4104		/*
4105		 * Chunk size starts at 1KB and is allowed to increase up to 1MB.
4106		 * These numbers are fairly arbitrary, though there is a hard limit at
4107		 * AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET; else we couldn't link event records to their
4108		 * shared records using the available space in ate_flags.  Another
4109		 * constraint is that if the chunk size gets too huge, the search loop
4110		 * below would get slow given a (not too common) usage pattern with
4111		 * many distinct event types in a chunk.  Therefore, we double the
4112		 * preceding chunk size only if there weren't too many shared records
4113		 * in the preceding chunk; otherwise we halve it.  This gives us some
4114		 * ability to adapt to the actual usage pattern of the current query
4115		 * while still having large chunk sizes in typical usage.  All chunk
4116		 * sizes used should be MAXALIGN multiples, to ensure that the shared
4117		 * records will be aligned safely.
4118		 */
4119#define MIN_CHUNK_SIZE 1024
4120#define MAX_CHUNK_SIZE (1024*1024)
4121
4122#if MAX_CHUNK_SIZE > (AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET+1)
4123#error MAX_CHUNK_SIZE must not exceed AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET
4124#endif
4125
4126		if (chunk == NULL)
4127			chunksize = MIN_CHUNK_SIZE;
4128		else
4129		{
4130			/* preceding chunk size... */
4131			chunksize = chunk->endptr - (char *) chunk;
4132			/* check number of shared records in preceding chunk */
4133			if ((chunk->endptr - chunk->endfree) <=
4134				(100 * sizeof(AfterTriggerSharedData)))
4135				chunksize *= 2; /* okay, double it */
4136			else
4137				chunksize /= 2; /* too many shared records */
4138			chunksize = Min(chunksize, MAX_CHUNK_SIZE);
4139		}
4140		chunk = MemoryContextAlloc(afterTriggers.event_cxt, chunksize);
4141		chunk->next = NULL;
4142		chunk->freeptr = CHUNK_DATA_START(chunk);
4143		chunk->endptr = chunk->endfree = (char *) chunk + chunksize;
4144		Assert(chunk->endfree - chunk->freeptr >= needed);
4145
4146		if (events->tail == NULL)
4147		{
4148			Assert(events->head == NULL);
4149			events->head = chunk;
4150		}
4151		else
4152			events->tail->next = chunk;
4153		events->tail = chunk;
4154		/* events->tailfree is now out of sync, but we'll fix it below */
4155	}
4156
4157	/*
4158	 * Try to locate a matching shared-data record already in the chunk. If
4159	 * none, make a new one. The search begins with the most recently added
4160	 * record, since newer ones are most likely to match.
4161	 */
4162	for (newshared = (AfterTriggerShared) chunk->endfree;
4163		 (char *) newshared < chunk->endptr;
4164		 newshared++)
4165	{
4166		/* compare fields roughly by probability of them being different */
4167		if (newshared->ats_tgoid == evtshared->ats_tgoid &&
4168			newshared->ats_event == evtshared->ats_event &&
4169			newshared->ats_firing_id == 0 &&
4170			newshared->ats_table == evtshared->ats_table &&
4171			newshared->ats_relid == evtshared->ats_relid &&
4172			newshared->ats_rolid == evtshared->ats_rolid &&
4173			bms_equal(newshared->ats_modifiedcols,
4174					  evtshared->ats_modifiedcols))
4175			break;
4176	}
4177	if ((char *) newshared >= chunk->endptr)
4178	{
4179		newshared = ((AfterTriggerShared) chunk->endfree) - 1;
4180		*newshared = *evtshared;
4181		/* now we must make a suitably-long-lived copy of the bitmap */
4182		newshared->ats_modifiedcols = afterTriggerCopyBitmap(evtshared->ats_modifiedcols);
4183		newshared->ats_firing_id = 0;	/* just to be sure */
4184		chunk->endfree = (char *) newshared;
4185	}
4186
4187	/* Insert the data */
4188	newevent = (AfterTriggerEvent) chunk->freeptr;
4189	memcpy(newevent, event, eventsize);
4190	/* ... and link the new event to its shared record */
4191	newevent->ate_flags &= ~AFTER_TRIGGER_OFFSET;
4192	newevent->ate_flags |= (char *) newshared - (char *) newevent;
4193
4194	chunk->freeptr += eventsize;
4195	events->tailfree = chunk->freeptr;
4196}
4197
4198/* ----------
4199 * afterTriggerFreeEventList()
4200 *
4201 *	Free all the event storage in the given list.
4202 * ----------
4203 */
4204static void
4205afterTriggerFreeEventList(AfterTriggerEventList *events)
4206{
4207	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
4208
4209	while ((chunk = events->head) != NULL)
4210	{
4211		events->head = chunk->next;
4212		pfree(chunk);
4213	}
4214	events->tail = NULL;
4215	events->tailfree = NULL;
4216}
4217
4218/* ----------
4219 * afterTriggerRestoreEventList()
4220 *
4221 *	Restore an event list to its prior length, removing all the events
4222 *	added since it had the value old_events.
4223 * ----------
4224 */
4225static void
4226afterTriggerRestoreEventList(AfterTriggerEventList *events,
4227							 const AfterTriggerEventList *old_events)
4228{
4229	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
4230	AfterTriggerEventChunk *next_chunk;
4231
4232	if (old_events->tail == NULL)
4233	{
4234		/* restoring to a completely empty state, so free everything */
4235		afterTriggerFreeEventList(events);
4236	}
4237	else
4238	{
4239		*events = *old_events;
4240		/* free any chunks after the last one we want to keep */
4241		for (chunk = events->tail->next; chunk != NULL; chunk = next_chunk)
4242		{
4243			next_chunk = chunk->next;
4244			pfree(chunk);
4245		}
4246		/* and clean up the tail chunk to be the right length */
4247		events->tail->next = NULL;
4248		events->tail->freeptr = events->tailfree;
4249
4250		/*
4251		 * We don't make any effort to remove now-unused shared data records.
4252		 * They might still be useful, anyway.
4253		 */
4254	}
4255}
4256
4257/* ----------
4258 * afterTriggerDeleteHeadEventChunk()
4259 *
4260 *	Remove the first chunk of events from the query level's event list.
4261 *	Keep any event list pointers elsewhere in the query level's data
4262 *	structures in sync.
4263 * ----------
4264 */
4265static void
4266afterTriggerDeleteHeadEventChunk(AfterTriggersQueryData *qs)
4267{
4268	AfterTriggerEventChunk *target = qs->events.head;
4269	ListCell   *lc;
4270
4271	Assert(target && target->next);
4272
4273	/*
4274	 * First, update any pointers in the per-table data, so that they won't be
4275	 * dangling.  Resetting obsoleted pointers to NULL will make
4276	 * cancel_prior_stmt_triggers start from the list head, which is fine.
4277	 */
4278	foreach(lc, qs->tables)
4279	{
4280		AfterTriggersTableData *table = (AfterTriggersTableData *) lfirst(lc);
4281
4282		if (table->after_trig_done &&
4283			table->after_trig_events.tail == target)
4284		{
4285			table->after_trig_events.head = NULL;
4286			table->after_trig_events.tail = NULL;
4287			table->after_trig_events.tailfree = NULL;
4288		}
4289	}
4290
4291	/* Now we can flush the head chunk */
4292	qs->events.head = target->next;
4293	pfree(target);
4294}
4295
4296
4297/* ----------
4298 * AfterTriggerExecute()
4299 *
4300 *	Fetch the required tuples back from the heap and fire one
4301 *	single trigger function.
4302 *
4303 *	Frequently, this will be fired many times in a row for triggers of
4304 *	a single relation.  Therefore, we cache the open relation and provide
4305 *	fmgr lookup cache space at the caller level.  (For triggers fired at
4306 *	the end of a query, we can even piggyback on the executor's state.)
4307 *
4308 *	When fired for a cross-partition update of a partitioned table, the old
4309 *	tuple is fetched using 'src_relInfo' (the source leaf partition) and
4310 *	the new tuple using 'dst_relInfo' (the destination leaf partition), though
4311 *	both are converted into the root partitioned table's format before passing
4312 *	to the trigger function.
4313 *
4314 *	event: event currently being fired.
4315 *	relInfo: result relation for event.
4316 *	src_relInfo: source partition of a cross-partition update
4317 *	dst_relInfo: its destination partition
4318 *	trigdesc: working copy of rel's trigger info.
4319 *	finfo: array of fmgr lookup cache entries (one per trigger in trigdesc).
4320 *	instr: array of EXPLAIN ANALYZE instrumentation nodes (one per trigger),
4321 *		or NULL if no instrumentation is wanted.
4322 *	per_tuple_context: memory context to call trigger function in.
4323 *	trig_tuple_slot1: scratch slot for tg_trigtuple (foreign tables only)
4324 *	trig_tuple_slot2: scratch slot for tg_newtuple (foreign tables only)
4325 * ----------
4326 */
4327static void
4328AfterTriggerExecute(EState *estate,
4329					AfterTriggerEvent event,
4330					ResultRelInfo *relInfo,
4331					ResultRelInfo *src_relInfo,
4332					ResultRelInfo *dst_relInfo,
4333					TriggerDesc *trigdesc,
4334					FmgrInfo *finfo, Instrumentation *instr,
4335					MemoryContext per_tuple_context,
4336					TupleTableSlot *trig_tuple_slot1,
4337					TupleTableSlot *trig_tuple_slot2)
4338{
4339	Relation	rel = relInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
4340	Relation	src_rel = src_relInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
4341	Relation	dst_rel = dst_relInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
4342	AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
4343	Oid			tgoid = evtshared->ats_tgoid;
4344	TriggerData LocTriggerData = {0};
4345	Oid			save_rolid;
4346	int			save_sec_context;
4347	HeapTuple	rettuple;
4348	int			tgindx;
4349	bool		should_free_trig = false;
4350	bool		should_free_new = false;
4351
4352	/*
4353	 * Locate trigger in trigdesc.  It might not be present, and in fact the
4354	 * trigdesc could be NULL, if the trigger was dropped since the event was
4355	 * queued.  In that case, silently do nothing.
4356	 */
4357	if (trigdesc == NULL)
4358		return;
4359	for (tgindx = 0; tgindx < trigdesc->numtriggers; tgindx++)
4360	{
4361		if (trigdesc->triggers[tgindx].tgoid == tgoid)
4362		{
4363			LocTriggerData.tg_trigger = &(trigdesc->triggers[tgindx]);
4364			break;
4365		}
4366	}
4367	if (LocTriggerData.tg_trigger == NULL)
4368		return;
4369
4370	/*
4371	 * If doing EXPLAIN ANALYZE, start charging time to this trigger. We want
4372	 * to include time spent re-fetching tuples in the trigger cost.
4373	 */
4374	if (instr)
4375		InstrStartNode(instr + tgindx);
4376
4377	/*
4378	 * Fetch the required tuple(s).
4379	 */
4380	switch (event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS)
4381	{
4382		case AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_FETCH:
4383			{
4384				Tuplestorestate *fdw_tuplestore = GetCurrentFDWTuplestore();
4385
4386				if (!tuplestore_gettupleslot(fdw_tuplestore, true, false,
4387											 trig_tuple_slot1))
4388					elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple1 for AFTER trigger");
4389
4390				if ((evtshared->ats_event & TRIGGER_EVENT_OPMASK) ==
4391					TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE &&
4392					!tuplestore_gettupleslot(fdw_tuplestore, true, false,
4393											 trig_tuple_slot2))
4394					elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple2 for AFTER trigger");
4395			}
4396			/* fall through */
4397		case AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_REUSE:
4398
4399			/*
4400			 * Store tuple in the slot so that tg_trigtuple does not reference
4401			 * tuplestore memory.  (It is formally possible for the trigger
4402			 * function to queue trigger events that add to the same
4403			 * tuplestore, which can push other tuples out of memory.)  The
4404			 * distinction is academic, because we start with a minimal tuple
4405			 * that is stored as a heap tuple, constructed in different memory
4406			 * context, in the slot anyway.
4407			 */
4408			LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = trig_tuple_slot1;
4409			LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple =
4410				ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(trig_tuple_slot1, true, &should_free_trig);
4411
4412			if ((evtshared->ats_event & TRIGGER_EVENT_OPMASK) ==
4413				TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE)
4414			{
4415				LocTriggerData.tg_newslot = trig_tuple_slot2;
4416				LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple =
4417					ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(trig_tuple_slot2, true, &should_free_new);
4418			}
4419			else
4420			{
4421				LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple = NULL;
4422			}
4423			break;
4424
4425		default:
4426			if (ItemPointerIsValid(&(event->ate_ctid1)))
4427			{
4428				TupleTableSlot *src_slot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate,
4429																 src_relInfo);
4430
4431				if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(src_rel,
4432												   &(event->ate_ctid1),
4433												   SnapshotAny,
4434												   src_slot))
4435					elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple1 for AFTER trigger");
4436
4437				/*
4438				 * Store the tuple fetched from the source partition into the
4439				 * target (root partitioned) table slot, converting if needed.
4440				 */
4441				if (src_relInfo != relInfo)
4442				{
4443					TupleConversionMap *map = ExecGetChildToRootMap(src_relInfo);
4444
4445					LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relInfo);
4446					if (map)
4447					{
4448						execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap,
4449											  src_slot,
4450											  LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot);
4451					}
4452					else
4453						ExecCopySlot(LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot, src_slot);
4454				}
4455				else
4456					LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot = src_slot;
4457				LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple =
4458					ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot, false, &should_free_trig);
4459			}
4460			else
4461			{
4462				LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple = NULL;
4463			}
4464
4465			/* don't touch ctid2 if not there */
4466			if (((event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS) == AFTER_TRIGGER_2CTID ||
4467				 (event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE)) &&
4468				ItemPointerIsValid(&(event->ate_ctid2)))
4469			{
4470				TupleTableSlot *dst_slot = ExecGetTriggerNewSlot(estate,
4471																 dst_relInfo);
4472
4473				if (!table_tuple_fetch_row_version(dst_rel,
4474												   &(event->ate_ctid2),
4475												   SnapshotAny,
4476												   dst_slot))
4477					elog(ERROR, "failed to fetch tuple2 for AFTER trigger");
4478
4479				/*
4480				 * Store the tuple fetched from the destination partition into
4481				 * the target (root partitioned) table slot, converting if
4482				 * needed.
4483				 */
4484				if (dst_relInfo != relInfo)
4485				{
4486					TupleConversionMap *map = ExecGetChildToRootMap(dst_relInfo);
4487
4488					LocTriggerData.tg_newslot = ExecGetTriggerNewSlot(estate, relInfo);
4489					if (map)
4490					{
4491						execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap,
4492											  dst_slot,
4493											  LocTriggerData.tg_newslot);
4494					}
4495					else
4496						ExecCopySlot(LocTriggerData.tg_newslot, dst_slot);
4497				}
4498				else
4499					LocTriggerData.tg_newslot = dst_slot;
4500				LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple =
4501					ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(LocTriggerData.tg_newslot, false, &should_free_new);
4502			}
4503			else
4504			{
4505				LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple = NULL;
4506			}
4507	}
4508
4509	/*
4510	 * Set up the tuplestore information to let the trigger have access to
4511	 * transition tables.  When we first make a transition table available to
4512	 * a trigger, mark it "closed" so that it cannot change anymore.  If any
4513	 * additional events of the same type get queued in the current trigger
4514	 * query level, they'll go into new transition tables.
4515	 */
4516	LocTriggerData.tg_oldtable = LocTriggerData.tg_newtable = NULL;
4517	if (evtshared->ats_table)
4518	{
4519		if (LocTriggerData.tg_trigger->tgoldtable)
4520		{
4521			LocTriggerData.tg_oldtable = evtshared->ats_table->old_tuplestore;
4522			evtshared->ats_table->closed = true;
4523		}
4524
4525		if (LocTriggerData.tg_trigger->tgnewtable)
4526		{
4527			LocTriggerData.tg_newtable = evtshared->ats_table->new_tuplestore;
4528			evtshared->ats_table->closed = true;
4529		}
4530	}
4531
4532	/*
4533	 * Setup the remaining trigger information
4534	 */
4535	LocTriggerData.type = T_TriggerData;
4536	LocTriggerData.tg_event =
4537		evtshared->ats_event & (TRIGGER_EVENT_OPMASK | TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW);
4538	LocTriggerData.tg_relation = rel;
4539	if (TRIGGER_FOR_UPDATE(LocTriggerData.tg_trigger->tgtype))
4540		LocTriggerData.tg_updatedcols = evtshared->ats_modifiedcols;
4541
4542	MemoryContextReset(per_tuple_context);
4543
4544	/*
4545	 * If necessary, become the role that was active when the trigger got
4546	 * queued.  Note that the role might have been dropped since the trigger
4547	 * was queued, but if that is a problem, we will get an error later.
4548	 * Checking here would still leave a race condition.
4549	 */
