4550	GetUserIdAndSecContext(&save_rolid, &save_sec_context);
4551	if (save_rolid != evtshared->ats_rolid)
4552		SetUserIdAndSecContext(evtshared->ats_rolid,
4553							   save_sec_context | SECURITY_LOCAL_USERID_CHANGE);
4554
4555	/*
4556	 * Call the trigger and throw away any possibly returned updated tuple.
4557	 * (Don't let ExecCallTriggerFunc measure EXPLAIN time.)
4558	 */
4559	rettuple = ExecCallTriggerFunc(&LocTriggerData,
4560								   tgindx,
4561								   finfo,
4562								   NULL,
4563								   per_tuple_context);
4564	if (rettuple != NULL &&
4565		rettuple != LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple &&
4566		rettuple != LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple)
4567		heap_freetuple(rettuple);
4568
4569	/* Restore the current role if necessary */
4570	if (save_rolid != evtshared->ats_rolid)
4571		SetUserIdAndSecContext(save_rolid, save_sec_context);
4572
4573	/*
4574	 * Release resources
4575	 */
4576	if (should_free_trig)
4577		heap_freetuple(LocTriggerData.tg_trigtuple);
4578	if (should_free_new)
4579		heap_freetuple(LocTriggerData.tg_newtuple);
4580
4581	/* don't clear slots' contents if foreign table */
4582	if (trig_tuple_slot1 == NULL)
4583	{
4584		if (LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot)
4585			ExecClearTuple(LocTriggerData.tg_trigslot);
4586		if (LocTriggerData.tg_newslot)
4587			ExecClearTuple(LocTriggerData.tg_newslot);
4588	}
4589
4590	/*
4591	 * If doing EXPLAIN ANALYZE, stop charging time to this trigger, and count
4592	 * one "tuple returned" (really the number of firings).
4593	 */
4594	if (instr)
4595		InstrStopNode(instr + tgindx, 1);
4596}
4597
4598
4599/*
4600 * afterTriggerMarkEvents()
4601 *
4602 *	Scan the given event list for not yet invoked events.  Mark the ones
4603 *	that can be invoked now with the current firing ID.
4604 *
4605 *	If move_list isn't NULL, events that are not to be invoked now are
4606 *	transferred to move_list.
4607 *
4608 *	When immediate_only is true, do not invoke currently-deferred triggers.
4609 *	(This will be false only at main transaction exit.)
4610 *
4611 *	Returns true if any invokable events were found.
4612 */
4613static bool
4614afterTriggerMarkEvents(AfterTriggerEventList *events,
4615					   AfterTriggerEventList *move_list,
4616					   bool immediate_only)
4617{
4618	bool		found = false;
4619	bool		deferred_found = false;
4620	AfterTriggerEvent event;
4621	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
4622
4623	for_each_event_chunk(event, chunk, *events)
4624	{
4625		AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
4626		bool		defer_it = false;
4627
4628		if (!(event->ate_flags &
4629			  (AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE | AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS)))
4630		{
4631			/*
4632			 * This trigger hasn't been called or scheduled yet. Check if we
4633			 * should call it now.
4634			 */
4635			if (immediate_only && afterTriggerCheckState(evtshared))
4636			{
4637				defer_it = true;
4638			}
4639			else
4640			{
4641				/*
4642				 * Mark it as to be fired in this firing cycle.
4643				 */
4644				evtshared->ats_firing_id = afterTriggers.firing_counter;
4645				event->ate_flags |= AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS;
4646				found = true;
4647			}
4648		}
4649
4650		/*
4651		 * If it's deferred, move it to move_list, if requested.
4652		 */
4653		if (defer_it && move_list != NULL)
4654		{
4655			deferred_found = true;
4656			/* add it to move_list */
4657			afterTriggerAddEvent(move_list, event, evtshared);
4658			/* mark original copy "done" so we don't do it again */
4659			event->ate_flags |= AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE;
4660		}
4661	}
4662
4663	/*
4664	 * We could allow deferred triggers if, before the end of the
4665	 * security-restricted operation, we were to verify that a SET CONSTRAINTS
4666	 * ... IMMEDIATE has fired all such triggers.  For now, don't bother.
4667	 */
4668	if (deferred_found && InSecurityRestrictedOperation())
4669		ereport(ERROR,
4670				(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
4671				 errmsg("cannot fire deferred trigger within security-restricted operation")));
4672
4673	return found;
4674}
4675
4676/*
4677 * afterTriggerInvokeEvents()
4678 *
4679 *	Scan the given event list for events that are marked as to be fired
4680 *	in the current firing cycle, and fire them.
4681 *
4682 *	If estate isn't NULL, we use its result relation info to avoid repeated
4683 *	openings and closing of trigger target relations.  If it is NULL, we
4684 *	make one locally to cache the info in case there are multiple trigger
4685 *	events per rel.
4686 *
4687 *	When delete_ok is true, it's safe to delete fully-processed events.
4688 *	(We are not very tense about that: we simply reset a chunk to be empty
4689 *	if all its events got fired.  The objective here is just to avoid useless
4690 *	rescanning of events when a trigger queues new events during transaction
4691 *	end, so it's not necessary to worry much about the case where only
4692 *	some events are fired.)
4693 *
4694 *	Returns true if no unfired events remain in the list (this allows us
4695 *	to avoid repeating afterTriggerMarkEvents).
4696 */
4697static bool
4698afterTriggerInvokeEvents(AfterTriggerEventList *events,
4699						 CommandId firing_id,
4700						 EState *estate,
4701						 bool delete_ok)
4702{
4703	bool		all_fired = true;
4704	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
4705	MemoryContext per_tuple_context;
4706	bool		local_estate = false;
4707	ResultRelInfo *rInfo = NULL;
4708	Relation	rel = NULL;
4709	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = NULL;
4710	FmgrInfo   *finfo = NULL;
4711	Instrumentation *instr = NULL;
4712	TupleTableSlot *slot1 = NULL,
4713			   *slot2 = NULL;
4714
4715	/* Make a local EState if need be */
4716	if (estate == NULL)
4717	{
4718		estate = CreateExecutorState();
4719		local_estate = true;
4720	}
4721
4722	/* Make a per-tuple memory context for trigger function calls */
4723	per_tuple_context =
4724		AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
4725							  "AfterTriggerTupleContext",
4726							  ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
4727
4728	for_each_chunk(chunk, *events)
4729	{
4730		AfterTriggerEvent event;
4731		bool		all_fired_in_chunk = true;
4732
4733		for_each_event(event, chunk)
4734		{
4735			AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
4736
4737			/*
4738			 * Is it one for me to fire?
4739			 */
4740			if ((event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS) &&
4741				evtshared->ats_firing_id == firing_id)
4742			{
4743				ResultRelInfo *src_rInfo,
4744						   *dst_rInfo;
4745
4746				/*
4747				 * So let's fire it... but first, find the correct relation if
4748				 * this is not the same relation as before.
4749				 */
4750				if (rel == NULL || RelationGetRelid(rel) != evtshared->ats_relid)
4751				{
4752					rInfo = ExecGetTriggerResultRel(estate, evtshared->ats_relid,
4753													NULL);
4754					rel = rInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
4755					/* Catch calls with insufficient relcache refcounting */
4756					Assert(!RelationHasReferenceCountZero(rel));
4757					trigdesc = rInfo->ri_TrigDesc;
4758					/* caution: trigdesc could be NULL here */
4759					finfo = rInfo->ri_TrigFunctions;
4760					instr = rInfo->ri_TrigInstrument;
4761					if (slot1 != NULL)
4762					{
4763						ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot1);
4764						ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot2);
4765						slot1 = slot2 = NULL;
4766					}
4767					if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
4768					{
4769						slot1 = MakeSingleTupleTableSlot(rel->rd_att,
4770														 &TTSOpsMinimalTuple);
4771						slot2 = MakeSingleTupleTableSlot(rel->rd_att,
4772														 &TTSOpsMinimalTuple);
4773					}
4774				}
4775
4776				/*
4777				 * Look up source and destination partition result rels of a
4778				 * cross-partition update event.
4779				 */
4780				if ((event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_TUP_BITS) ==
4781					AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE)
4782				{
4783					Assert(OidIsValid(event->ate_src_part) &&
4784						   OidIsValid(event->ate_dst_part));
4785					src_rInfo = ExecGetTriggerResultRel(estate,
4786														event->ate_src_part,
4787														rInfo);
4788					dst_rInfo = ExecGetTriggerResultRel(estate,
4789														event->ate_dst_part,
4790														rInfo);
4791				}
4792				else
4793					src_rInfo = dst_rInfo = rInfo;
4794
4795				/*
4796				 * Fire it.  Note that the AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS flag is
4797				 * still set, so recursive examinations of the event list
4798				 * won't try to re-fire it.
4799				 */
4800				AfterTriggerExecute(estate, event, rInfo,
4801									src_rInfo, dst_rInfo,
4802									trigdesc, finfo, instr,
4803									per_tuple_context, slot1, slot2);
4804
4805				/*
4806				 * Mark the event as done.
4807				 */
4808				event->ate_flags &= ~AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS;
4809				event->ate_flags |= AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE;
4810			}
4811			else if (!(event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE))
4812			{
4813				/* something remains to be done */
4814				all_fired = all_fired_in_chunk = false;
4815			}
4816		}
4817
4818		/* Clear the chunk if delete_ok and nothing left of interest */
4819		if (delete_ok && all_fired_in_chunk)
4820		{
4821			chunk->freeptr = CHUNK_DATA_START(chunk);
4822			chunk->endfree = chunk->endptr;
4823
4824			/*
4825			 * If it's last chunk, must sync event list's tailfree too.  Note
4826			 * that delete_ok must NOT be passed as true if there could be
4827			 * additional AfterTriggerEventList values pointing at this event
4828			 * list, since we'd fail to fix their copies of tailfree.
4829			 */
4830			if (chunk == events->tail)
4831				events->tailfree = chunk->freeptr;
4832		}
4833	}
4834	if (slot1 != NULL)
4835	{
4836		ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot1);
4837		ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot2);
4838	}
4839
4840	/* Release working resources */
4841	MemoryContextDelete(per_tuple_context);
4842
4843	if (local_estate)
4844	{
4845		ExecCloseResultRelations(estate);
4846		ExecResetTupleTable(estate->es_tupleTable, false);
4847		FreeExecutorState(estate);
4848	}
4849
4850	return all_fired;
4851}
4852
4853
4854/*
4855 * GetAfterTriggersTableData
4856 *
4857 * Find or create an AfterTriggersTableData struct for the specified
4858 * trigger event (relation + operation type).  Ignore existing structs
4859 * marked "closed"; we don't want to put any additional tuples into them,
4860 * nor change their stmt-triggers-fired state.
4861 *
4862 * Note: the AfterTriggersTableData list is allocated in the current
4863 * (sub)transaction's CurTransactionContext.  This is OK because
4864 * we don't need it to live past AfterTriggerEndQuery.
4865 */
4866static AfterTriggersTableData *
4867GetAfterTriggersTableData(Oid relid, CmdType cmdType)
4868{
4869	AfterTriggersTableData *table;
4870	AfterTriggersQueryData *qs;
4871	MemoryContext oldcxt;
4872	ListCell   *lc;
4873
4874	/* At this level, cmdType should not be, eg, CMD_MERGE */
4875	Assert(cmdType == CMD_INSERT ||
4876		   cmdType == CMD_UPDATE ||
4877		   cmdType == CMD_DELETE);
4878
4879	/* Caller should have ensured query_depth is OK. */
4880	Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth >= 0 &&
4881		   afterTriggers.query_depth < afterTriggers.maxquerydepth);
4882	qs = &afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth];
4883
4884	foreach(lc, qs->tables)
4885	{
4886		table = (AfterTriggersTableData *) lfirst(lc);
4887		if (table->relid == relid && table->cmdType == cmdType &&
4888			!table->closed)
4889			return table;
4890	}
4891
4892	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CurTransactionContext);
4893
4894	table = (AfterTriggersTableData *) palloc0(sizeof(AfterTriggersTableData));
4895	table->relid = relid;
4896	table->cmdType = cmdType;
4897	qs->tables = lappend(qs->tables, table);
4898
4899	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4900
4901	return table;
4902}
4903
4904/*
4905 * Returns a TupleTableSlot suitable for holding the tuples to be put
4906 * into AfterTriggersTableData's transition table tuplestores.
4907 */
4908static TupleTableSlot *
4909GetAfterTriggersStoreSlot(AfterTriggersTableData *table,
4910						  TupleDesc tupdesc)
4911{
4912	/* Create it if not already done. */
4913	if (!table->storeslot)
4914	{
4915		MemoryContext oldcxt;
4916
4917		/*
4918		 * We need this slot only until AfterTriggerEndQuery, but making it
4919		 * last till end-of-subxact is good enough.  It'll be freed by
4920		 * AfterTriggerFreeQuery().  However, the passed-in tupdesc might have
4921		 * a different lifespan, so we'd better make a copy of that.
4922		 */
4923		oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CurTransactionContext);
4924		tupdesc = CreateTupleDescCopy(tupdesc);
4925		table->storeslot = MakeSingleTupleTableSlot(tupdesc, &TTSOpsVirtual);
4926		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
4927	}
4928
4929	return table->storeslot;
4930}
4931
4932/*
4933 * MakeTransitionCaptureState
4934 *
4935 * Make a TransitionCaptureState object for the given TriggerDesc, target
4936 * relation, and operation type.  The TCS object holds all the state needed
4937 * to decide whether to capture tuples in transition tables.
4938 *
4939 * If there are no triggers in 'trigdesc' that request relevant transition
4940 * tables, then return NULL.
4941 *
4942 * The resulting object can be passed to the ExecAR* functions.  When
4943 * dealing with child tables, the caller can set tcs_original_insert_tuple
4944 * to avoid having to reconstruct the original tuple in the root table's
4945 * format.
4946 *
4947 * Note that we copy the flags from a parent table into this struct (rather
4948 * than subsequently using the relation's TriggerDesc directly) so that we can
4949 * use it to control collection of transition tuples from child tables.
4950 *
4951 * Per SQL spec, all operations of the same kind (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
4952 * on the same table during one query should share one transition table.
4953 * Therefore, the Tuplestores are owned by an AfterTriggersTableData struct
4954 * looked up using the table OID + CmdType, and are merely referenced by
4955 * the TransitionCaptureState objects we hand out to callers.
4956 */
4957TransitionCaptureState *
4958MakeTransitionCaptureState(TriggerDesc *trigdesc, Oid relid, CmdType cmdType)
4959{
4960	TransitionCaptureState *state;
4961	bool		need_old_upd,
4962				need_new_upd,
4963				need_old_del,
4964				need_new_ins;
4965	AfterTriggersTableData *ins_table;
4966	AfterTriggersTableData *upd_table;
4967	AfterTriggersTableData *del_table;
4968	MemoryContext oldcxt;
4969	ResourceOwner saveResourceOwner;
4970
4971	if (trigdesc == NULL)
4972		return NULL;
4973
4974	/* Detect which table(s) we need. */
4975	switch (cmdType)
4976	{
4977		case CMD_INSERT:
4978			need_old_upd = need_old_del = need_new_upd = false;
4979			need_new_ins = trigdesc->trig_insert_new_table;
4980			break;
4981		case CMD_UPDATE:
4982			need_old_upd = trigdesc->trig_update_old_table;
4983			need_new_upd = trigdesc->trig_update_new_table;
4984			need_old_del = need_new_ins = false;
4985			break;
4986		case CMD_DELETE:
4987			need_old_del = trigdesc->trig_delete_old_table;
4988			need_old_upd = need_new_upd = need_new_ins = false;
4989			break;
4990		case CMD_MERGE:
4991			need_old_upd = trigdesc->trig_update_old_table;
4992			need_new_upd = trigdesc->trig_update_new_table;
4993			need_old_del = trigdesc->trig_delete_old_table;
4994			need_new_ins = trigdesc->trig_insert_new_table;
4995			break;
4996		default:
4997			elog(ERROR, "unexpected CmdType: %d", (int) cmdType);
4998			/* keep compiler quiet */
4999			need_old_upd = need_new_upd = need_old_del = need_new_ins = false;
5000			break;
5001	}
5002	if (!need_old_upd && !need_new_upd && !need_new_ins && !need_old_del)
5003		return NULL;
5004
5005	/* Check state, like AfterTriggerSaveEvent. */
5006	if (afterTriggers.query_depth < 0)
5007		elog(ERROR, "MakeTransitionCaptureState() called outside of query");
5008
5009	/* Be sure we have enough space to record events at this query depth. */
5010	if (afterTriggers.query_depth >= afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
5011		AfterTriggerEnlargeQueryState();
5012
5013	/*
5014	 * Find or create AfterTriggersTableData struct(s) to hold the
5015	 * tuplestore(s).  If there's a matching struct but it's marked closed,
5016	 * ignore it; we need a newer one.
5017	 *
5018	 * Note: MERGE must use the same AfterTriggersTableData structs as INSERT,
5019	 * UPDATE, and DELETE, so that any MERGE'd tuples are added to the same
5020	 * tuplestores as tuples from any INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE commands
5021	 * running in the same top-level command (e.g., in a writable CTE).
5022	 *
5023	 * Note: the AfterTriggersTableData list, as well as the tuplestores, are
5024	 * allocated in the current (sub)transaction's CurTransactionContext, and
5025	 * the tuplestores are managed by the (sub)transaction's resource owner.
5026	 * This is sufficient lifespan because we do not allow triggers using
5027	 * transition tables to be deferrable; they will be fired during
5028	 * AfterTriggerEndQuery, after which it's okay to delete the data.
5029	 */
5030	if (need_new_ins)
5031		ins_table = GetAfterTriggersTableData(relid, CMD_INSERT);
5032	else
5033		ins_table = NULL;
5034
5035	if (need_old_upd || need_new_upd)
5036		upd_table = GetAfterTriggersTableData(relid, CMD_UPDATE);
5037	else
5038		upd_table = NULL;
5039
5040	if (need_old_del)
5041		del_table = GetAfterTriggersTableData(relid, CMD_DELETE);
5042	else
5043		del_table = NULL;
5044
5045	/* Now create required tuplestore(s), if we don't have them already. */
5046	oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CurTransactionContext);
5047	saveResourceOwner = CurrentResourceOwner;
5048	CurrentResourceOwner = CurTransactionResourceOwner;
5049
5050	if (need_old_upd && upd_table->old_tuplestore == NULL)
5051		upd_table->old_tuplestore = tuplestore_begin_heap(false, false, work_mem);
5052	if (need_new_upd && upd_table->new_tuplestore == NULL)
5053		upd_table->new_tuplestore = tuplestore_begin_heap(false, false, work_mem);
5054	if (need_old_del && del_table->old_tuplestore == NULL)
5055		del_table->old_tuplestore = tuplestore_begin_heap(false, false, work_mem);
5056	if (need_new_ins && ins_table->new_tuplestore == NULL)
5057		ins_table->new_tuplestore = tuplestore_begin_heap(false, false, work_mem);
5058
5059	CurrentResourceOwner = saveResourceOwner;
5060	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
5061
5062	/* Now build the TransitionCaptureState struct, in caller's context */
5063	state = (TransitionCaptureState *) palloc0(sizeof(TransitionCaptureState));
5064	state->tcs_delete_old_table = need_old_del;
5065	state->tcs_update_old_table = need_old_upd;
5066	state->tcs_update_new_table = need_new_upd;
5067	state->tcs_insert_new_table = need_new_ins;
5068	state->tcs_insert_private = ins_table;
5069	state->tcs_update_private = upd_table;
5070	state->tcs_delete_private = del_table;
5071
5072	return state;
5073}
5074
5075
5076/* ----------
5077 * AfterTriggerBeginXact()
5078 *
5079 *	Called at transaction start (either BEGIN or implicit for single
5080 *	statement outside of transaction block).
5081 * ----------
5082 */
5083void
5084AfterTriggerBeginXact(void)
5085{
5086	/*
5087	 * Initialize after-trigger state structure to empty
5088	 */
5089	afterTriggers.firing_counter = (CommandId) 1;	/* mustn't be 0 */
5090	afterTriggers.query_depth = -1;
5091
5092	/*
5093	 * Verify that there is no leftover state remaining.  If these assertions
5094	 * trip, it means that AfterTriggerEndXact wasn't called or didn't clean
5095	 * up properly.
5096	 */
5097	Assert(afterTriggers.state == NULL);
5098	Assert(afterTriggers.query_stack == NULL);
5099	Assert(afterTriggers.maxquerydepth == 0);
5100	Assert(afterTriggers.event_cxt == NULL);
5101	Assert(afterTriggers.events.head == NULL);
5102	Assert(afterTriggers.trans_stack == NULL);
5103	Assert(afterTriggers.maxtransdepth == 0);
5104}
5105
5106
5107/* ----------
5108 * AfterTriggerBeginQuery()
5109 *
5110 *	Called just before we start processing a single query within a
5111 *	transaction (or subtransaction).  Most of the real work gets deferred
5112 *	until somebody actually tries to queue a trigger event.
5113 * ----------
5114 */
5115void
5116AfterTriggerBeginQuery(void)
5117{
5118	/* Increase the query stack depth */
5119	afterTriggers.query_depth++;
5120}
5121
5122
5123/* ----------
5124 * AfterTriggerEndQuery()
5125 *
5126 *	Called after one query has been completely processed. At this time
5127 *	we invoke all AFTER IMMEDIATE trigger events queued by the query, and
5128 *	transfer deferred trigger events to the global deferred-trigger list.
5129 *
5130 *	Note that this must be called BEFORE closing down the executor
5131 *	with ExecutorEnd, because we make use of the EState's info about
5132 *	target relations.  Normally it is called from ExecutorFinish.
5133 * ----------
5134 */
5135void
5136AfterTriggerEndQuery(EState *estate)
5137{
5138	AfterTriggersQueryData *qs;
5139
5140	/* Must be inside a query, too */
5141	Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth >= 0);
5142
5143	/*
5144	 * If we never even got as far as initializing the event stack, there
5145	 * certainly won't be any events, so exit quickly.
5146	 */
5147	if (afterTriggers.query_depth >= afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
5148	{
5149		afterTriggers.query_depth--;
5150		return;
5151	}
5152
5153	/*
5154	 * Process all immediate-mode triggers queued by the query, and move the
5155	 * deferred ones to the main list of deferred events.
5156	 *
5157	 * Notice that we decide which ones will be fired, and put the deferred
5158	 * ones on the main list, before anything is actually fired.  This ensures
5159	 * reasonably sane behavior if a trigger function does SET CONSTRAINTS ...
5160	 * IMMEDIATE: all events we have decided to defer will be available for it
5161	 * to fire.
5162	 *
5163	 * We loop in case a trigger queues more events at the same query level.
5164	 * Ordinary trigger functions, including all PL/pgSQL trigger functions,
5165	 * will instead fire any triggers in a dedicated query level.  Foreign key
5166	 * enforcement triggers do add to the current query level, thanks to their
5167	 * passing fire_triggers = false to SPI_execute_snapshot().  Other
5168	 * C-language triggers might do likewise.
5169	 *
5170	 * If we find no firable events, we don't have to increment
5171	 * firing_counter.
5172	 */
5173	qs = &afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth];
5174
5175	for (;;)
5176	{
5177		if (afterTriggerMarkEvents(&qs->events, &afterTriggers.events, true))
5178		{
5179			CommandId	firing_id = afterTriggers.firing_counter++;
5180			AfterTriggerEventChunk *oldtail = qs->events.tail;
5181
5182			if (afterTriggerInvokeEvents(&qs->events, firing_id, estate, false))
5183				break;			/* all fired */
5184
5185			/*
5186			 * Firing a trigger could result in query_stack being repalloc'd,
5187			 * so we must recalculate qs after each afterTriggerInvokeEvents
5188			 * call.  Furthermore, it's unsafe to pass delete_ok = true here,
5189			 * because that could cause afterTriggerInvokeEvents to try to
5190			 * access qs->events after the stack has been repalloc'd.
5191			 */
5192			qs = &afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth];
5193
5194			/*
5195			 * We'll need to scan the events list again.  To reduce the cost
5196			 * of doing so, get rid of completely-fired chunks.  We know that
5197			 * all events were marked IN_PROGRESS or DONE at the conclusion of
5198			 * afterTriggerMarkEvents, so any still-interesting events must
5199			 * have been added after that, and so must be in the chunk that
5200			 * was then the tail chunk, or in later chunks.  So, zap all
5201			 * chunks before oldtail.  This is approximately the same set of
5202			 * events we would have gotten rid of by passing delete_ok = true.
5203			 */
5204			Assert(oldtail != NULL);
5205			while (qs->events.head != oldtail)
5206				afterTriggerDeleteHeadEventChunk(qs);
5207		}
5208		else
5209			break;
5210	}
5211
5212	/* Release query-level-local storage, including tuplestores if any */
5213	AfterTriggerFreeQuery(&afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth]);
5214
5215	afterTriggers.query_depth--;
5216}
5217
5218
5219/*
5220 * AfterTriggerFreeQuery
5221 *	Release subsidiary storage for a trigger query level.
5222 *	This includes closing down tuplestores.
5223 *	Note: it's important for this to be safe if interrupted by an error
5224 *	and then called again for the same query level.
5225 */
5226static void
5227AfterTriggerFreeQuery(AfterTriggersQueryData *qs)
5228{
5229	Tuplestorestate *ts;
5230	List	   *tables;
5231	ListCell   *lc;
5232
5233	/* Drop the trigger events */
5234	afterTriggerFreeEventList(&qs->events);
5235
5236	/* Drop FDW tuplestore if any */
5237	ts = qs->fdw_tuplestore;
5238	qs->fdw_tuplestore = NULL;
5239	if (ts)
5240		tuplestore_end(ts);
5241
5242	/* Release per-table subsidiary storage */
5243	tables = qs->tables;
5244	foreach(lc, tables)
5245	{
5246		AfterTriggersTableData *table = (AfterTriggersTableData *) lfirst(lc);
5247
5248		ts = table->old_tuplestore;
5249		table->old_tuplestore = NULL;
5250		if (ts)
5251			tuplestore_end(ts);
5252		ts = table->new_tuplestore;
5253		table->new_tuplestore = NULL;
5254		if (ts)
5255			tuplestore_end(ts);
5256		if (table->storeslot)
5257		{
5258			TupleTableSlot *slot = table->storeslot;
5259
5260			table->storeslot = NULL;
5261			ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot);
5262		}
5263	}
5264
5265	/*
5266	 * Now free the AfterTriggersTableData structs and list cells.  Reset list
5267	 * pointer first; if list_free_deep somehow gets an error, better to leak
5268	 * that storage than have an infinite loop.
5269	 */
5270	qs->tables = NIL;
5271	list_free_deep(tables);
5272}
5273
5274
5275/* ----------
5276 * AfterTriggerFireDeferred()
5277 *
5278 *	Called just before the current transaction is committed. At this
5279 *	time we invoke all pending DEFERRED triggers.
5280 *
5281 *	It is possible for other modules to queue additional deferred triggers
5282 *	during pre-commit processing; therefore xact.c may have to call this
5283 *	multiple times.
5284 * ----------
5285 */
5286void
5287AfterTriggerFireDeferred(void)
5288{
5289	AfterTriggerEventList *events;
5290	bool		snap_pushed = false;
5291
5292	/* Must not be inside a query */
5293	Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth == -1);
5294
5295	/*
5296	 * If there are any triggers to fire, make sure we have set a snapshot for
5297	 * them to use.  (Since PortalRunUtility doesn't set a snap for COMMIT, we
5298	 * can't assume ActiveSnapshot is valid on entry.)
5299	 */
5300	events = &afterTriggers.events;
5301	if (events->head != NULL)
5302	{
5303		PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
5304		snap_pushed = true;
5305	}
5306
5307	/*
5308	 * Run all the remaining triggers.  Loop until they are all gone, in case
5309	 * some trigger queues more for us to do.
5310	 */
5311	while (afterTriggerMarkEvents(events, NULL, false))
5312	{
5313		CommandId	firing_id = afterTriggers.firing_counter++;
5314
5315		if (afterTriggerInvokeEvents(events, firing_id, NULL, true))
5316			break;				/* all fired */
5317	}
5318
5319	/*
5320	 * We don't bother freeing the event list, since it will go away anyway
5321	 * (and more efficiently than via pfree) in AfterTriggerEndXact.
5322	 */
5323
5324	if (snap_pushed)
5325		PopActiveSnapshot();
5326}
5327
5328
5329/* ----------
5330 * AfterTriggerEndXact()
5331 *
5332 *	The current transaction is finishing.
5333 *
5334 *	Any unfired triggers are canceled so we simply throw
5335 *	away anything we know.
5336 *
5337 *	Note: it is possible for this to be called repeatedly in case of
5338 *	error during transaction abort; therefore, do not complain if
5339 *	already closed down.
5340 * ----------
5341 */
5342void
5343AfterTriggerEndXact(bool isCommit)
5344{
5345	/*
5346	 * Forget the pending-events list.
5347	 *
5348	 * Since all the info is in TopTransactionContext or children thereof, we
5349	 * don't really need to do anything to reclaim memory.  However, the
5350	 * pending-events list could be large, and so it's useful to discard it as
5351	 * soon as possible --- especially if we are aborting because we ran out
5352	 * of memory for the list!
5353	 */
5354	if (afterTriggers.event_cxt)
5355	{
5356		MemoryContextDelete(afterTriggers.event_cxt);
5357		afterTriggers.event_cxt = NULL;
5358		afterTriggers.events.head = NULL;
5359		afterTriggers.events.tail = NULL;
5360		afterTriggers.events.tailfree = NULL;
5361	}
5362
5363	/*
5364	 * Forget any subtransaction state as well.  Since this can't be very
5365	 * large, we let the eventual reset of TopTransactionContext free the
5366	 * memory instead of doing it here.
5367	 */
5368	afterTriggers.trans_stack = NULL;
5369	afterTriggers.maxtransdepth = 0;
5370
5371
5372	/*
5373	 * Forget the query stack and constraint-related state information.  As
5374	 * with the subtransaction state information, we don't bother freeing the
5375	 * memory here.
5376	 */
5377	afterTriggers.query_stack = NULL;
5378	afterTriggers.maxquerydepth = 0;
5379	afterTriggers.state = NULL;
5380
5381	/* No more afterTriggers manipulation until next transaction starts. */
5382	afterTriggers.query_depth = -1;
5383}
5384
5385/*
5386 * AfterTriggerBeginSubXact()
5387 *
5388 *	Start a subtransaction.
5389 */
5390void
5391AfterTriggerBeginSubXact(void)
5392{
5393	int			my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
5394
5395	/*
5396	 * Allocate more space in the trans_stack if needed.  (Note: because the
5397	 * minimum nest level of a subtransaction is 2, we waste the first couple
5398	 * entries of the array; not worth the notational effort to avoid it.)
5399	 */
5400	while (my_level >= afterTriggers.maxtransdepth)
5401	{
5402		if (afterTriggers.maxtransdepth == 0)
5403		{
5404			/* Arbitrarily initialize for max of 8 subtransaction levels */
5405			afterTriggers.trans_stack = (AfterTriggersTransData *)
5406				MemoryContextAlloc(TopTransactionContext,
5407								   8 * sizeof(AfterTriggersTransData));
5408			afterTriggers.maxtransdepth = 8;
5409		}
5410		else
5411		{
5412			/* repalloc will keep the stack in the same context */
5413			int			new_alloc = afterTriggers.maxtransdepth * 2;
5414
5415			afterTriggers.trans_stack = (AfterTriggersTransData *)
5416				repalloc(afterTriggers.trans_stack,
5417						 new_alloc * sizeof(AfterTriggersTransData));
5418			afterTriggers.maxtransdepth = new_alloc;
5419		}
5420	}
5421
5422	/*
5423	 * Push the current information into the stack.  The SET CONSTRAINTS state
5424	 * is not saved until/unless changed.  Likewise, we don't make a
5425	 * per-subtransaction event context until needed.
5426	 */
5427	afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state = NULL;
5428	afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].events = afterTriggers.events;
5429	afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].query_depth = afterTriggers.query_depth;
5430	afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].firing_counter = afterTriggers.firing_counter;
5431}
5432
5433/*
5434 * AfterTriggerEndSubXact()
5435 *
5436 *	The current subtransaction is ending.
5437 */
5438void
5439AfterTriggerEndSubXact(bool isCommit)
5440{
5441	int			my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
5442	SetConstraintState state;
5443	AfterTriggerEvent event;
5444	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
5445	CommandId	subxact_firing_id;
5446
5447	/*
5448	 * Pop the prior state if needed.
5449	 */
5450	if (isCommit)
5451	{
5452		Assert(my_level < afterTriggers.maxtransdepth);
5453		/* If we saved a prior state, we don't need it anymore */
5454		state = afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state;
5455		if (state != NULL)
5456			pfree(state);
5457		/* this avoids double pfree if error later: */
5458		afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state = NULL;
5459		Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth ==
5460			   afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].query_depth);
5461	}
5462	else
5463	{
5464		/*
5465		 * Aborting.  It is possible subxact start failed before calling
5466		 * AfterTriggerBeginSubXact, in which case we mustn't risk touching
5467		 * trans_stack levels that aren't there.
5468		 */
5469		if (my_level >= afterTriggers.maxtransdepth)
5470			return;
5471
5472		/*
5473		 * Release query-level storage for queries being aborted, and restore
5474		 * query_depth to its pre-subxact value.  This assumes that a
5475		 * subtransaction will not add events to query levels started in a
5476		 * earlier transaction state.
5477		 */
5478		while (afterTriggers.query_depth > afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].query_depth)
5479		{
5480			if (afterTriggers.query_depth < afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
5481				AfterTriggerFreeQuery(&afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth]);
5482			afterTriggers.query_depth--;
5483		}
5484		Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth ==
5485			   afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].query_depth);
5486
5487		/*
5488		 * Restore the global deferred-event list to its former length,
5489		 * discarding any events queued by the subxact.
5490		 */
5491		afterTriggerRestoreEventList(&afterTriggers.events,
5492									 &afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].events);
5493
5494		/*
5495		 * Restore the trigger state.  If the saved state is NULL, then this
5496		 * subxact didn't save it, so it doesn't need restoring.
5497		 */
5498		state = afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state;
5499		if (state != NULL)
5500		{
5501			pfree(afterTriggers.state);
5502			afterTriggers.state = state;
5503		}
5504		/* this avoids double pfree if error later: */
5505		afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state = NULL;
5506
5507		/*
5508		 * Scan for any remaining deferred events that were marked DONE or IN
5509		 * PROGRESS by this subxact or a child, and un-mark them. We can
5510		 * recognize such events because they have a firing ID greater than or
5511		 * equal to the firing_counter value we saved at subtransaction start.
5512		 * (This essentially assumes that the current subxact includes all
5513		 * subxacts started after it.)
5514		 */
5515		subxact_firing_id = afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].firing_counter;
5516		for_each_event_chunk(event, chunk, afterTriggers.events)
5517		{
5518			AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
5519
5520			if (event->ate_flags &
5521				(AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE | AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS))
5522			{
5523				if (evtshared->ats_firing_id >= subxact_firing_id)
5524					event->ate_flags &=
5525						~(AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE | AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS);
5526			}
5527		}
5528	}
5529}
5530
5531/*
5532 * Get the transition table for the given event and depending on whether we are
5533 * processing the old or the new tuple.
5534 */
5535static Tuplestorestate *
5536GetAfterTriggersTransitionTable(int event,
5537								TupleTableSlot *oldslot,
5538								TupleTableSlot *newslot,
5539								TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture)
5540{
5541	Tuplestorestate *tuplestore = NULL;
5542	bool		delete_old_table = transition_capture->tcs_delete_old_table;
5543	bool		update_old_table = transition_capture->tcs_update_old_table;
5544	bool		update_new_table = transition_capture->tcs_update_new_table;
5545	bool		insert_new_table = transition_capture->tcs_insert_new_table;
5546
5547	/*
5548	 * For INSERT events NEW should be non-NULL, for DELETE events OLD should
5549	 * be non-NULL, whereas for UPDATE events normally both OLD and NEW are
5550	 * non-NULL.  But for UPDATE events fired for capturing transition tuples
5551	 * during UPDATE partition-key row movement, OLD is NULL when the event is
5552	 * for a row being inserted, whereas NEW is NULL when the event is for a
5553	 * row being deleted.
5554	 */
5555	Assert(!(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE && delete_old_table &&
5556			 TupIsNull(oldslot)));
5557	Assert(!(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT && insert_new_table &&
5558			 TupIsNull(newslot)));
5559
5560	if (!TupIsNull(oldslot))
5561	{
5562		Assert(TupIsNull(newslot));
5563		if (event == TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE && delete_old_table)
5564			tuplestore = transition_capture->tcs_delete_private->old_tuplestore;
5565		else if (event == TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE && update_old_table)
5566			tuplestore = transition_capture->tcs_update_private->old_tuplestore;
5567	}
5568	else if (!TupIsNull(newslot))
5569	{
5570		Assert(TupIsNull(oldslot));
5571		if (event == TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT && insert_new_table)
5572			tuplestore = transition_capture->tcs_insert_private->new_tuplestore;
5573		else if (event == TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE && update_new_table)
5574			tuplestore = transition_capture->tcs_update_private->new_tuplestore;
5575	}
5576
5577	return tuplestore;
5578}
5579
5580/*
5581 * Add the given heap tuple to the given tuplestore, applying the conversion
5582 * map if necessary.
5583 *
5584 * If original_insert_tuple is given, we can add that tuple without conversion.
5585 */
5586static void
5587TransitionTableAddTuple(EState *estate,
5588						int event,
5589						TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture,
5590						ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
5591						TupleTableSlot *slot,
5592						TupleTableSlot *original_insert_tuple,
5593						Tuplestorestate *tuplestore)
5594{
5595	TupleConversionMap *map;
5596
5597	/*
5598	 * Nothing needs to be done if we don't have a tuplestore.
5599	 */
5600	if (tuplestore == NULL)
5601		return;
5602
5603	if (original_insert_tuple)
5604		tuplestore_puttupleslot(tuplestore, original_insert_tuple);
5605	else if ((map = ExecGetChildToRootMap(relinfo)) != NULL)
5606	{
5607		AfterTriggersTableData *table;
5608		TupleTableSlot *storeslot;
5609
5610		switch (event)
5611		{
5612			case TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT:
5613				table = transition_capture->tcs_insert_private;
5614				break;
5615			case TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE:
5616				table = transition_capture->tcs_update_private;
5617				break;
5618			case TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE:
5619				table = transition_capture->tcs_delete_private;
5620				break;
5621			default:
5622				elog(ERROR, "invalid after-trigger event code: %d", event);
5623				table = NULL;	/* keep compiler quiet */
5624				break;
5625		}
5626
5627		storeslot = GetAfterTriggersStoreSlot(table, map->outdesc);
5628		execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap, slot, storeslot);
5629		tuplestore_puttupleslot(tuplestore, storeslot);
5630	}
5631	else
5632		tuplestore_puttupleslot(tuplestore, slot);
5633}
5634
5635/* ----------
5636 * AfterTriggerEnlargeQueryState()
5637 *
5638 *	Prepare the necessary state so that we can record AFTER trigger events
5639 *	queued by a query.  It is allowed to have nested queries within a
5640 *	(sub)transaction, so we need to have separate state for each query
5641 *	nesting level.
5642 * ----------
5643 */
5644static void
5645AfterTriggerEnlargeQueryState(void)
5646{
5647	int			init_depth = afterTriggers.maxquerydepth;
5648
5649	Assert(afterTriggers.query_depth >= afterTriggers.maxquerydepth);
5650
5651	if (afterTriggers.maxquerydepth == 0)
5652	{
5653		int			new_alloc = Max(afterTriggers.query_depth + 1, 8);
5654
5655		afterTriggers.query_stack = (AfterTriggersQueryData *)
5656			MemoryContextAlloc(TopTransactionContext,
5657							   new_alloc * sizeof(AfterTriggersQueryData));
5658		afterTriggers.maxquerydepth = new_alloc;
5659	}
5660	else
5661	{
5662		/* repalloc will keep the stack in the same context */
5663		int			old_alloc = afterTriggers.maxquerydepth;
5664		int			new_alloc = Max(afterTriggers.query_depth + 1,
5665									old_alloc * 2);
5666
5667		afterTriggers.query_stack = (AfterTriggersQueryData *)
5668			repalloc(afterTriggers.query_stack,
5669					 new_alloc * sizeof(AfterTriggersQueryData));
5670		afterTriggers.maxquerydepth = new_alloc;
5671	}
5672
5673	/* Initialize new array entries to empty */
5674	while (init_depth < afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
5675	{
5676		AfterTriggersQueryData *qs = &afterTriggers.query_stack[init_depth];
5677
5678		qs->events.head = NULL;
5679		qs->events.tail = NULL;
5680		qs->events.tailfree = NULL;
5681		qs->fdw_tuplestore = NULL;
5682		qs->tables = NIL;
5683
5684		++init_depth;
5685	}
5686}
5687
5688/*
5689 * Create an empty SetConstraintState with room for numalloc trigstates
5690 */
5691static SetConstraintState
5692SetConstraintStateCreate(int numalloc)
5693{
5694	SetConstraintState state;
5695
5696	/* Behave sanely with numalloc == 0 */
5697	if (numalloc <= 0)
5698		numalloc = 1;
5699
5700	/*
5701	 * We assume that zeroing will correctly initialize the state values.
5702	 */
5703	state = (SetConstraintState)
5704		MemoryContextAllocZero(TopTransactionContext,
5705							   offsetof(SetConstraintStateData, trigstates) +
5706							   numalloc * sizeof(SetConstraintTriggerData));
5707
5708	state->numalloc = numalloc;
5709
5710	return state;
5711}
5712
5713/*
5714 * Copy a SetConstraintState
5715 */
5716static SetConstraintState
5717SetConstraintStateCopy(SetConstraintState origstate)
5718{
5719	SetConstraintState state;
5720
5721	state = SetConstraintStateCreate(origstate->numstates);
5722
5723	state->all_isset = origstate->all_isset;
5724	state->all_isdeferred = origstate->all_isdeferred;
5725	state->numstates = origstate->numstates;
5726	memcpy(state->trigstates, origstate->trigstates,
5727		   origstate->numstates * sizeof(SetConstraintTriggerData));
5728
5729	return state;
5730}
5731
5732/*
5733 * Add a per-trigger item to a SetConstraintState.  Returns possibly-changed
5734 * pointer to the state object (it will change if we have to repalloc).
5735 */
5736static SetConstraintState
5737SetConstraintStateAddItem(SetConstraintState state,
5738						  Oid tgoid, bool tgisdeferred)
5739{
5740	if (state->numstates >= state->numalloc)
5741	{
5742		int			newalloc = state->numalloc * 2;
5743
5744		newalloc = Max(newalloc, 8);	/* in case original has size 0 */
5745		state = (SetConstraintState)
5746			repalloc(state,
5747					 offsetof(SetConstraintStateData, trigstates) +
5748					 newalloc * sizeof(SetConstraintTriggerData));
5749		state->numalloc = newalloc;
5750		Assert(state->numstates < state->numalloc);
5751	}
5752
5753	state->trigstates[state->numstates].sct_tgoid = tgoid;
5754	state->trigstates[state->numstates].sct_tgisdeferred = tgisdeferred;
5755	state->numstates++;
5756
5757	return state;
5758}
5759
5760/* ----------
5761 * AfterTriggerSetState()
5762 *
5763 *	Execute the SET CONSTRAINTS ... utility command.
5764 * ----------
5765 */
5766void
5767AfterTriggerSetState(ConstraintsSetStmt *stmt)
5768{
5769	int			my_level = GetCurrentTransactionNestLevel();
5770
5771	/* If we haven't already done so, initialize our state. */
5772	if (afterTriggers.state == NULL)
5773		afterTriggers.state = SetConstraintStateCreate(8);
5774
5775	/*
5776	 * If in a subtransaction, and we didn't save the current state already,
5777	 * save it so it can be restored if the subtransaction aborts.
5778	 */
5779	if (my_level > 1 &&
5780		afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state == NULL)
5781	{
5782		afterTriggers.trans_stack[my_level].state =
5783			SetConstraintStateCopy(afterTriggers.state);
5784	}
5785
5786	/*
5787	 * Handle SET CONSTRAINTS ALL ...
5788	 */
5789	if (stmt->constraints == NIL)
5790	{
5791		/*
5792		 * Forget any previous SET CONSTRAINTS commands in this transaction.
5793		 */
5794		afterTriggers.state->numstates = 0;
5795
5796		/*
5797		 * Set the per-transaction ALL state to known.
5798		 */
5799		afterTriggers.state->all_isset = true;
5800		afterTriggers.state->all_isdeferred = stmt->deferred;
5801	}
5802	else
5803	{
5804		Relation	conrel;
5805		Relation	tgrel;
5806		List	   *conoidlist = NIL;
5807		List	   *tgoidlist = NIL;
5808		ListCell   *lc;
5809
5810		/*
5811		 * Handle SET CONSTRAINTS constraint-name [, ...]
5812		 *
5813		 * First, identify all the named constraints and make a list of their
5814		 * OIDs.  Since, unlike the SQL spec, we allow multiple constraints of
5815		 * the same name within a schema, the specifications are not
5816		 * necessarily unique.  Our strategy is to target all matching
5817		 * constraints within the first search-path schema that has any
5818		 * matches, but disregard matches in schemas beyond the first match.
5819		 * (This is a bit odd but it's the historical behavior.)
5820		 *
5821		 * A constraint in a partitioned table may have corresponding
5822		 * constraints in the partitions.  Grab those too.
5823		 */
5824		conrel = table_open(ConstraintRelationId, AccessShareLock);
5825
5826		foreach(lc, stmt->constraints)
5827		{
5828			RangeVar   *constraint = lfirst(lc);
5829			bool		found;
5830			List	   *namespacelist;
5831			ListCell   *nslc;
5832
5833			if (constraint->catalogname)
5834			{
5835				if (strcmp(constraint->catalogname, get_database_name(MyDatabaseId)) != 0)
5836					ereport(ERROR,
5837							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
5838							 errmsg("cross-database references are not implemented: \"%s.%s.%s\"",
5839									constraint->catalogname, constraint->schemaname,
5840									constraint->relname)));
5841			}
5842
5843			/*
5844			 * If we're given the schema name with the constraint, look only
5845			 * in that schema.  If given a bare constraint name, use the
5846			 * search path to find the first matching constraint.
5847			 */
5848			if (constraint->schemaname)
5849			{
5850				Oid			namespaceId = LookupExplicitNamespace(constraint->schemaname,
5851																  false);
5852
5853				namespacelist = list_make1_oid(namespaceId);
5854			}
5855			else
5856			{
5857				namespacelist = fetch_search_path(true);
5858			}
5859
5860			found = false;
5861			foreach(nslc, namespacelist)
5862			{
5863				Oid			namespaceId = lfirst_oid(nslc);
5864				SysScanDesc conscan;
5865				ScanKeyData skey[2];
5866				HeapTuple	tup;
5867
5868				ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
5869							Anum_pg_constraint_conname,
5870							BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_NAMEEQ,
5871							CStringGetDatum(constraint->relname));
5872				ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],
5873							Anum_pg_constraint_connamespace,
5874							BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
5875							ObjectIdGetDatum(namespaceId));
5876
5877				conscan = systable_beginscan(conrel, ConstraintNameNspIndexId,
5878											 true, NULL, 2, skey);
5879
5880				while (HeapTupleIsValid(tup = systable_getnext(conscan)))
5881				{
5882					Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tup);
5883
5884					if (con->condeferrable)
5885						conoidlist = lappend_oid(conoidlist, con->oid);
5886					else if (stmt->deferred)
5887						ereport(ERROR,
5888								(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
5889								 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" is not deferrable",
5890										constraint->relname)));
5891					found = true;
5892				}
5893
5894				systable_endscan(conscan);
5895
5896				/*
5897				 * Once we've found a matching constraint we do not search
5898				 * later parts of the search path.
5899				 */
5900				if (found)
5901					break;
5902			}
5903
5904			list_free(namespacelist);
5905
5906			/*
5907			 * Not found ?
5908			 */
5909			if (!found)
5910				ereport(ERROR,
5911						(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
5912						 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" does not exist",
5913								constraint->relname)));
5914		}
5915
5916		/*
5917		 * Scan for any possible descendants of the constraints.  We append
5918		 * whatever we find to the same list that we're scanning; this has the
5919		 * effect that we create new scans for those, too, so if there are
5920		 * further descendents, we'll also catch them.
5921		 */
5922		foreach(lc, conoidlist)
5923		{
5924			Oid			parent = lfirst_oid(lc);
5925			ScanKeyData key;
5926			SysScanDesc scan;
5927			HeapTuple	tuple;
5928
5929			ScanKeyInit(&key,
5930						Anum_pg_constraint_conparentid,
5931						BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
5932						ObjectIdGetDatum(parent));
5933
5934			scan = systable_beginscan(conrel, ConstraintParentIndexId, true, NULL, 1, &key);
5935
5936			while (HeapTupleIsValid(tuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
5937			{
5938				Form_pg_constraint con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
5939
5940				conoidlist = lappend_oid(conoidlist, con->oid);
5941			}
5942
5943			systable_endscan(scan);
5944		}
5945
5946		table_close(conrel, AccessShareLock);
5947
5948		/*
5949		 * Now, locate the trigger(s) implementing each of these constraints,
5950		 * and make a list of their OIDs.
5951		 */
5952		tgrel = table_open(TriggerRelationId, AccessShareLock);
5953
5954		foreach(lc, conoidlist)
5955		{
5956			Oid			conoid = lfirst_oid(lc);
5957			ScanKeyData skey;
5958			SysScanDesc tgscan;
5959			HeapTuple	htup;
5960
5961			ScanKeyInit(&skey,
5962						Anum_pg_trigger_tgconstraint,
5963						BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
5964						ObjectIdGetDatum(conoid));
5965
5966			tgscan = systable_beginscan(tgrel, TriggerConstraintIndexId, true,
5967										NULL, 1, &skey);
5968
5969			while (HeapTupleIsValid(htup = systable_getnext(tgscan)))
5970			{
5971				Form_pg_trigger pg_trigger = (Form_pg_trigger) GETSTRUCT(htup);
5972
5973				/*
5974				 * Silently skip triggers that are marked as non-deferrable in
5975				 * pg_trigger.  This is not an error condition, since a
5976				 * deferrable RI constraint may have some non-deferrable
5977				 * actions.
5978				 */
5979				if (pg_trigger->tgdeferrable)
5980					tgoidlist = lappend_oid(tgoidlist, pg_trigger->oid);
5981			}
5982
5983			systable_endscan(tgscan);
5984		}
5985
5986		table_close(tgrel, AccessShareLock);
5987
5988		/*
5989		 * Now we can set the trigger states of individual triggers for this
5990		 * xact.
5991		 */
5992		foreach(lc, tgoidlist)
5993		{
5994			Oid			tgoid = lfirst_oid(lc);
5995			SetConstraintState state = afterTriggers.state;
5996			bool		found = false;
5997			int			i;
5998
5999			for (i = 0; i < state->numstates; i++)
6000			{
6001				if (state->trigstates[i].sct_tgoid == tgoid)
6002				{
6003					state->trigstates[i].sct_tgisdeferred = stmt->deferred;
6004					found = true;
6005					break;
6006				}
6007			}
6008			if (!found)
6009			{
6010				afterTriggers.state =
6011					SetConstraintStateAddItem(state, tgoid, stmt->deferred);
6012			}
6013		}
6014	}
6015
6016	/*
6017	 * SQL99 requires that when a constraint is set to IMMEDIATE, any deferred
6018	 * checks against that constraint must be made when the SET CONSTRAINTS
6019	 * command is executed -- i.e. the effects of the SET CONSTRAINTS command
6020	 * apply retroactively.  We've updated the constraints state, so scan the
6021	 * list of previously deferred events to fire any that have now become
6022	 * immediate.
6023	 *
6024	 * Obviously, if this was SET ... DEFERRED then it can't have converted
6025	 * any unfired events to immediate, so we need do nothing in that case.
6026	 */
6027	if (!stmt->deferred)
6028	{
6029		AfterTriggerEventList *events = &afterTriggers.events;
6030		bool		snapshot_set = false;
6031
6032		while (afterTriggerMarkEvents(events, NULL, true))
6033		{
6034			CommandId	firing_id = afterTriggers.firing_counter++;
6035
6036			/*
6037			 * Make sure a snapshot has been established in case trigger
6038			 * functions need one.  Note that we avoid setting a snapshot if
6039			 * we don't find at least one trigger that has to be fired now.
6040			 * This is so that BEGIN; SET CONSTRAINTS ...; SET TRANSACTION
6041			 * ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE; ... works properly.  (If we are
6042			 * at the start of a transaction it's not possible for any trigger
6043			 * events to be queued yet.)
6044			 */
6045			if (!snapshot_set)
6046			{
6047				PushActiveSnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
6048				snapshot_set = true;
6049			}
6050
6051			/*
6052			 * We can delete fired events if we are at top transaction level,
6053			 * but we'd better not if inside a subtransaction, since the
6054			 * subtransaction could later get rolled back.
6055			 */
6056			if (afterTriggerInvokeEvents(events, firing_id, NULL,
6057										 !IsSubTransaction()))
6058				break;			/* all fired */
6059		}
6060
6061		if (snapshot_set)
6062			PopActiveSnapshot();
6063	}
6064}
6065
6066/* ----------
6067 * AfterTriggerPendingOnRel()
6068 *		Test to see if there are any pending after-trigger events for rel.
6069 *
6070 * This is used by TRUNCATE, CLUSTER, ALTER TABLE, etc to detect whether
6071 * it is unsafe to perform major surgery on a relation.  Note that only
6072 * local pending events are examined.  We assume that having exclusive lock
6073 * on a rel guarantees there are no unserviced events in other backends ---
6074 * but having a lock does not prevent there being such events in our own.
6075 *
6076 * In some scenarios it'd be reasonable to remove pending events (more
6077 * specifically, mark them DONE by the current subxact) but without a lot
6078 * of knowledge of the trigger semantics we can't do this in general.
6079 * ----------
6080 */
6081bool
6082AfterTriggerPendingOnRel(Oid relid)
6083{
6084	AfterTriggerEvent event;
6085	AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
6086	int			depth;
6087
6088	/* Scan queued events */
6089	for_each_event_chunk(event, chunk, afterTriggers.events)
6090	{
6091		AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
6092
6093		/*
6094		 * We can ignore completed events.  (Even if a DONE flag is rolled
6095		 * back by subxact abort, it's OK because the effects of the TRUNCATE
6096		 * or whatever must get rolled back too.)
6097		 */
6098		if (event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE)
6099			continue;
6100
6101		if (evtshared->ats_relid == relid)
6102			return true;
6103	}
6104
6105	/*
6106	 * Also scan events queued by incomplete queries.  This could only matter
6107	 * if TRUNCATE/etc is executed by a function or trigger within an updating
6108	 * query on the same relation, which is pretty perverse, but let's check.
6109	 */
6110	for (depth = 0; depth <= afterTriggers.query_depth && depth < afterTriggers.maxquerydepth; depth++)
6111	{
6112		for_each_event_chunk(event, chunk, afterTriggers.query_stack[depth].events)
6113		{
6114			AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
6115
6116			if (event->ate_flags & AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE)
6117				continue;
6118
6119			if (evtshared->ats_relid == relid)
6120				return true;
6121		}
6122	}
6123
6124	return false;
6125}
6126
6127/* ----------
6128 * AfterTriggerSaveEvent()
6129 *
6130 *	Called by ExecA[RS]...Triggers() to queue up the triggers that should
6131 *	be fired for an event.
6132 *
6133 *	NOTE: this is called whenever there are any triggers associated with
6134 *	the event (even if they are disabled).  This function decides which
6135 *	triggers actually need to be queued.  It is also called after each row,
6136 *	even if there are no triggers for that event, if there are any AFTER
6137 *	STATEMENT triggers for the statement which use transition tables, so that
6138 *	the transition tuplestores can be built.  Furthermore, if the transition
6139 *	capture is happening for UPDATEd rows being moved to another partition due
6140 *	to the partition-key being changed, then this function is called once when
6141 *	the row is deleted (to capture OLD row), and once when the row is inserted
6142 *	into another partition (to capture NEW row).  This is done separately because
6143 *	DELETE and INSERT happen on different tables.
6144 *
6145 *	Transition tuplestores are built now, rather than when events are pulled
6146 *	off of the queue because AFTER ROW triggers are allowed to select from the
6147 *	transition tables for the statement.
6148 *
6149 *	This contains special support to queue the update events for the case where
6150 *	a partitioned table undergoing a cross-partition update may have foreign
6151 *	keys pointing into it.  Normally, a partitioned table's row triggers are
6152 *	not fired because the leaf partition(s) which are modified as a result of
6153 *	the operation on the partitioned table contain the same triggers which are
6154 *	fired instead. But that general scheme can cause problematic behavior with
6155 *	foreign key triggers during cross-partition updates, which are implemented
6156 *	as DELETE on the source partition followed by INSERT into the destination
6157 *	partition.  Specifically, firing DELETE triggers would lead to the wrong
6158 *	foreign key action to be enforced considering that the original command is
6159 *	UPDATE; in this case, this function is called with relinfo as the
6160 *	partitioned table, and src_partinfo and dst_partinfo referring to the
6161 *	source and target leaf partitions, respectively.
6162 *
6163 *	is_crosspart_update is true either when a DELETE event is fired on the
6164 *	source partition (which is to be ignored) or an UPDATE event is fired on
6165 *	the root partitioned table.
6166 * ----------
6167 */
6168static void
6169AfterTriggerSaveEvent(EState *estate, ResultRelInfo *relinfo,
6170					  ResultRelInfo *src_partinfo,
6171					  ResultRelInfo *dst_partinfo,
6172					  int event, bool row_trigger,
6173					  TupleTableSlot *oldslot, TupleTableSlot *newslot,
6174					  List *recheckIndexes, Bitmapset *modifiedCols,
6175					  TransitionCaptureState *transition_capture,
6176					  bool is_crosspart_update)
6177{
6178	Relation	rel = relinfo->ri_RelationDesc;
6179	TriggerDesc *trigdesc = relinfo->ri_TrigDesc;
6180	AfterTriggerEventData new_event;
6181	AfterTriggerSharedData new_shared;
6182	char		relkind = rel->rd_rel->relkind;
6183	int			tgtype_event;
6184	int			tgtype_level;
6185	int			i;
6186	Tuplestorestate *fdw_tuplestore = NULL;
6187
6188	/*
6189	 * Check state.  We use a normal test not Assert because it is possible to
6190	 * reach here in the wrong state given misconfigured RI triggers, in
6191	 * particular deferring a cascade action trigger.
6192	 */
6193	if (afterTriggers.query_depth < 0)
6194		elog(ERROR, "AfterTriggerSaveEvent() called outside of query");
6195
6196	/* Be sure we have enough space to record events at this query depth. */
6197	if (afterTriggers.query_depth >= afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
6198		AfterTriggerEnlargeQueryState();
6199
6200	/*
6201	 * If the directly named relation has any triggers with transition tables,
6202	 * then we need to capture transition tuples.
6203	 */
6204	if (row_trigger && transition_capture != NULL)
6205	{
6206		TupleTableSlot *original_insert_tuple = transition_capture->tcs_original_insert_tuple;
6207
6208		/*
6209		 * Capture the old tuple in the appropriate transition table based on
6210		 * the event.
6211		 */
6212		if (!TupIsNull(oldslot))
6213		{
6214			Tuplestorestate *old_tuplestore;
6215
6216			old_tuplestore = GetAfterTriggersTransitionTable(event,
6217															 oldslot,
6218															 NULL,
6219															 transition_capture);
6220			TransitionTableAddTuple(estate, event, transition_capture, relinfo,
6221									oldslot, NULL, old_tuplestore);
6222		}
6223
6224		/*
6225		 * Capture the new tuple in the appropriate transition table based on
6226		 * the event.
6227		 */
6228		if (!TupIsNull(newslot))
6229		{
6230			Tuplestorestate *new_tuplestore;
6231
6232			new_tuplestore = GetAfterTriggersTransitionTable(event,
6233															 NULL,
6234															 newslot,
6235															 transition_capture);
6236			TransitionTableAddTuple(estate, event, transition_capture, relinfo,
6237									newslot, original_insert_tuple, new_tuplestore);
6238		}
6239
6240		/*
6241		 * If transition tables are the only reason we're here, return. As
6242		 * mentioned above, we can also be here during update tuple routing in
6243		 * presence of transition tables, in which case this function is
6244		 * called separately for OLD and NEW, so we expect exactly one of them
6245		 * to be NULL.
6246		 */
6247		if (trigdesc == NULL ||
6248			(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE && !trigdesc->trig_delete_after_row) ||
6249			(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT && !trigdesc->trig_insert_after_row) ||
6250			(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE && !trigdesc->trig_update_after_row) ||
6251			(event == TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE && (TupIsNull(oldslot) ^ TupIsNull(newslot))))
6252			return;
6253	}
6254
6255	/*
6256	 * We normally don't see partitioned tables here for row level triggers
6257	 * except in the special case of a cross-partition update.  In that case,
6258	 * nodeModifyTable.c:ExecCrossPartitionUpdateForeignKey() calls here to
6259	 * queue an update event on the root target partitioned table, also
6260	 * passing the source and destination partitions and their tuples.
6261	 */
6262	Assert(!row_trigger ||
6263		   rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE ||
6264		   (is_crosspart_update &&
6265			TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_UPDATE(event) &&
6266			src_partinfo != NULL && dst_partinfo != NULL));
6267
6268	/*
6269	 * Validate the event code and collect the associated tuple CTIDs.
6270	 *
6271	 * The event code will be used both as a bitmask and an array offset, so
6272	 * validation is important to make sure we don't walk off the edge of our
6273	 * arrays.
6274	 *
6275	 * Also, if we're considering statement-level triggers, check whether we
6276	 * already queued a set of them for this event, and cancel the prior set
6277	 * if so.  This preserves the behavior that statement-level triggers fire
6278	 * just once per statement and fire after row-level triggers.
6279	 */
6280	switch (event)
6281	{
6282		case TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT:
6283			tgtype_event = TRIGGER_TYPE_INSERT;
6284			if (row_trigger)
6285			{
6286				Assert(oldslot == NULL);
6287				Assert(newslot != NULL);
6288				ItemPointerCopy(&(newslot->tts_tid), &(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6289				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6290			}
6291			else
6292			{
6293				Assert(oldslot == NULL);
6294				Assert(newslot == NULL);
6295				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6296				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6297				cancel_prior_stmt_triggers(RelationGetRelid(rel),
6298										   CMD_INSERT, event);
6299			}
6300			break;
6301		case TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE:
6302			tgtype_event = TRIGGER_TYPE_DELETE;
6303			if (row_trigger)
6304			{
6305				Assert(oldslot != NULL);
6306				Assert(newslot == NULL);
6307				ItemPointerCopy(&(oldslot->tts_tid), &(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6308				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6309			}
6310			else
6311			{
6312				Assert(oldslot == NULL);
6313				Assert(newslot == NULL);
6314				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6315				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6316				cancel_prior_stmt_triggers(RelationGetRelid(rel),
6317										   CMD_DELETE, event);
6318			}
6319			break;
6320		case TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE:
6321			tgtype_event = TRIGGER_TYPE_UPDATE;
6322			if (row_trigger)
6323			{
6324				Assert(oldslot != NULL);
6325				Assert(newslot != NULL);
6326				ItemPointerCopy(&(oldslot->tts_tid), &(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6327				ItemPointerCopy(&(newslot->tts_tid), &(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6328
6329				/*
6330				 * Also remember the OIDs of partitions to fetch these tuples
6331				 * out of later in AfterTriggerExecute().
6332				 */
6333				if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
6334				{
6335					Assert(src_partinfo != NULL && dst_partinfo != NULL);
6336					new_event.ate_src_part =
6337						RelationGetRelid(src_partinfo->ri_RelationDesc);
6338					new_event.ate_dst_part =
6339						RelationGetRelid(dst_partinfo->ri_RelationDesc);
6340				}
6341			}
6342			else
6343			{
6344				Assert(oldslot == NULL);
6345				Assert(newslot == NULL);
6346				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6347				ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6348				cancel_prior_stmt_triggers(RelationGetRelid(rel),
6349										   CMD_UPDATE, event);
6350			}
6351			break;
6352		case TRIGGER_EVENT_TRUNCATE:
6353			tgtype_event = TRIGGER_TYPE_TRUNCATE;
6354			Assert(oldslot == NULL);
6355			Assert(newslot == NULL);
6356			ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid1));
6357			ItemPointerSetInvalid(&(new_event.ate_ctid2));
6358			break;
6359		default:
6360			elog(ERROR, "invalid after-trigger event code: %d", event);
6361			tgtype_event = 0;	/* keep compiler quiet */
6362			break;
6363	}
6364
6365	/* Determine flags */
6366	if (!(relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE && row_trigger))
6367	{
6368		if (row_trigger && event == TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE)
6369		{
6370			if (relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
6371				new_event.ate_flags = AFTER_TRIGGER_CP_UPDATE;
6372			else
6373				new_event.ate_flags = AFTER_TRIGGER_2CTID;
6374		}
6375		else
6376			new_event.ate_flags = AFTER_TRIGGER_1CTID;
6377	}
6378
6379	/* else, we'll initialize ate_flags for each trigger */
6380
6381	tgtype_level = (row_trigger ? TRIGGER_TYPE_ROW : TRIGGER_TYPE_STATEMENT);
6382
6383	/*
6384	 * Must convert/copy the source and destination partition tuples into the
6385	 * root partitioned table's format/slot, because the processing in the
6386	 * loop below expects both oldslot and newslot tuples to be in that form.
6387	 */
6388	if (row_trigger && rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
6389	{
6390		TupleTableSlot *rootslot;
6391		TupleConversionMap *map;
6392
6393		rootslot = ExecGetTriggerOldSlot(estate, relinfo);
6394		map = ExecGetChildToRootMap(src_partinfo);
6395		if (map)
6396			oldslot = execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap,
6397											oldslot,
6398											rootslot);
6399		else
6400			oldslot = ExecCopySlot(rootslot, oldslot);
6401
6402		rootslot = ExecGetTriggerNewSlot(estate, relinfo);
6403		map = ExecGetChildToRootMap(dst_partinfo);
6404		if (map)
6405			newslot = execute_attr_map_slot(map->attrMap,
6406											newslot,
6407											rootslot);
6408		else
6409			newslot = ExecCopySlot(rootslot, newslot);
6410	}
6411
6412	for (i = 0; i < trigdesc->numtriggers; i++)
6413	{
6414		Trigger    *trigger = &trigdesc->triggers[i];
6415
6416		if (!TRIGGER_TYPE_MATCHES(trigger->tgtype,
6417								  tgtype_level,
6418								  TRIGGER_TYPE_AFTER,
6419								  tgtype_event))
6420			continue;
6421		if (!TriggerEnabled(estate, relinfo, trigger, event,
6422							modifiedCols, oldslot, newslot))
6423			continue;
6424
6425		if (relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE && row_trigger)
6426		{
6427			if (fdw_tuplestore == NULL)
6428			{
6429				fdw_tuplestore = GetCurrentFDWTuplestore();
6430				new_event.ate_flags = AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_FETCH;
6431			}
6432			else
6433				/* subsequent event for the same tuple */
6434				new_event.ate_flags = AFTER_TRIGGER_FDW_REUSE;
6435		}
6436
6437		/*
6438		 * If the trigger is a foreign key enforcement trigger, there are
6439		 * certain cases where we can skip queueing the event because we can
6440		 * tell by inspection that the FK constraint will still pass. There
6441		 * are also some cases during cross-partition updates of a partitioned
6442		 * table where queuing the event can be skipped.
6443		 */
6444		if (TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_UPDATE(event) || TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_DELETE(event))
6445		{
6446			switch (RI_FKey_trigger_type(trigger->tgfoid))
6447			{
6448				case RI_TRIGGER_PK:
6449
6450					/*
6451					 * For cross-partitioned updates of partitioned PK table,
6452					 * skip the event fired by the component delete on the
6453					 * source leaf partition unless the constraint originates
6454					 * in the partition itself (!tgisclone), because the
6455					 * update event that will be fired on the root
6456					 * (partitioned) target table will be used to perform the
6457					 * necessary foreign key enforcement action.
6458					 */
6459					if (is_crosspart_update &&
6460						TRIGGER_FIRED_BY_DELETE(event) &&
6461						trigger->tgisclone)
6462						continue;
6463
6464					/* Update or delete on trigger's PK table */
6465					if (!RI_FKey_pk_upd_check_required(trigger, rel,
6466													   oldslot, newslot))
6467					{
6468						/* skip queuing this event */
6469						continue;
6470					}
6471					break;
6472
6473				case RI_TRIGGER_FK:
6474
6475					/*
6476					 * Update on trigger's FK table.  We can skip the update
6477					 * event fired on a partitioned table during a
6478					 * cross-partition of that table, because the insert event
6479					 * that is fired on the destination leaf partition would
6480					 * suffice to perform the necessary foreign key check.
6481					 * Moreover, RI_FKey_fk_upd_check_required() expects to be
6482					 * passed a tuple that contains system attributes, most of
6483					 * which are not present in the virtual slot belonging to
6484					 * a partitioned table.
6485					 */
6486					if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE ||
6487						!RI_FKey_fk_upd_check_required(trigger, rel,
6488													   oldslot, newslot))
6489					{
6490						/* skip queuing this event */
6491						continue;
6492					}
6493					break;
6494
6495				case RI_TRIGGER_NONE:
6496
6497					/*
6498					 * Not an FK trigger.  No need to queue the update event
6499					 * fired during a cross-partitioned update of a
6500					 * partitioned table, because the same row trigger must be
6501					 * present in the leaf partition(s) that are affected as
6502					 * part of this update and the events fired on them are
6503					 * queued instead.
6504					 */
6505					if (row_trigger &&
6506						rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
6507						continue;
6508					break;
6509			}
6510		}
6511
6512		/*
6513		 * If the trigger is a deferred unique constraint check trigger, only
6514		 * queue it if the unique constraint was potentially violated, which
6515		 * we know from index insertion time.
6516		 */
6517		if (trigger->tgfoid == F_UNIQUE_KEY_RECHECK)
6518		{
6519			if (!list_member_oid(recheckIndexes, trigger->tgconstrindid))
6520				continue;		/* Uniqueness definitely not violated */
6521		}
6522
6523		/*
6524		 * Fill in event structure and add it to the current query's queue.
6525		 * Note we set ats_table to NULL whenever this trigger doesn't use
6526		 * transition tables, to improve sharability of the shared event data.
6527		 */
6528		new_shared.ats_event =
6529			(event & TRIGGER_EVENT_OPMASK) |
6530			(row_trigger ? TRIGGER_EVENT_ROW : 0) |
6531			(trigger->tgdeferrable ? AFTER_TRIGGER_DEFERRABLE : 0) |
6532			(trigger->tginitdeferred ? AFTER_TRIGGER_INITDEFERRED : 0);
6533		new_shared.ats_tgoid = trigger->tgoid;
6534		new_shared.ats_relid = RelationGetRelid(rel);
6535		new_shared.ats_rolid = GetUserId();
6536		new_shared.ats_firing_id = 0;
6537		if ((trigger->tgoldtable || trigger->tgnewtable) &&
6538			transition_capture != NULL)
6539		{
6540			switch (event)
6541			{
6542				case TRIGGER_EVENT_INSERT:
6543					new_shared.ats_table = transition_capture->tcs_insert_private;
6544					break;
6545				case TRIGGER_EVENT_UPDATE:
6546					new_shared.ats_table = transition_capture->tcs_update_private;
6547					break;
6548				case TRIGGER_EVENT_DELETE:
6549					new_shared.ats_table = transition_capture->tcs_delete_private;
6550					break;
6551				default:
6552					/* Must be TRUNCATE, see switch above */
6553					new_shared.ats_table = NULL;
6554					break;
6555			}
6556		}
6557		else
6558			new_shared.ats_table = NULL;
6559		new_shared.ats_modifiedcols = modifiedCols;
6560
6561		afterTriggerAddEvent(&afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth].events,
6562							 &new_event, &new_shared);
6563	}
6564
6565	/*
6566	 * Finally, spool any foreign tuple(s).  The tuplestore squashes them to
6567	 * minimal tuples, so this loses any system columns.  The executor lost
6568	 * those columns before us, for an unrelated reason, so this is fine.
6569	 */
6570	if (fdw_tuplestore)
6571	{
6572		if (oldslot != NULL)
6573			tuplestore_puttupleslot(fdw_tuplestore, oldslot);
6574		if (newslot != NULL)
6575			tuplestore_puttupleslot(fdw_tuplestore, newslot);
6576	}
6577}
6578
6579/*
6580 * Detect whether we already queued BEFORE STATEMENT triggers for the given
6581 * relation + operation, and set the flag so the next call will report "true".
6582 */
6583static bool
6584before_stmt_triggers_fired(Oid relid, CmdType cmdType)
6585{
6586	bool		result;
6587	AfterTriggersTableData *table;
6588
6589	/* Check state, like AfterTriggerSaveEvent. */
6590	if (afterTriggers.query_depth < 0)
6591		elog(ERROR, "before_stmt_triggers_fired() called outside of query");
6592
6593	/* Be sure we have enough space to record events at this query depth. */
6594	if (afterTriggers.query_depth >= afterTriggers.maxquerydepth)
6595		AfterTriggerEnlargeQueryState();
6596
6597	/*
6598	 * We keep this state in the AfterTriggersTableData that also holds
6599	 * transition tables for the relation + operation.  In this way, if we are
6600	 * forced to make a new set of transition tables because more tuples get
6601	 * entered after we've already fired triggers, we will allow a new set of
6602	 * statement triggers to get queued.
6603	 */
6604	table = GetAfterTriggersTableData(relid, cmdType);
6605	result = table->before_trig_done;
6606	table->before_trig_done = true;
6607	return result;
6608}
6609
6610/*
6611 * If we previously queued a set of AFTER STATEMENT triggers for the given
6612 * relation + operation, and they've not been fired yet, cancel them.  The
6613 * caller will queue a fresh set that's after any row-level triggers that may
6614 * have been queued by the current sub-statement, preserving (as much as
6615 * possible) the property that AFTER ROW triggers fire before AFTER STATEMENT
6616 * triggers, and that the latter only fire once.  This deals with the
6617 * situation where several FK enforcement triggers sequentially queue triggers
6618 * for the same table into the same trigger query level.  We can't fully
6619 * prevent odd behavior though: if there are AFTER ROW triggers taking
6620 * transition tables, we don't want to change the transition tables once the
6621 * first such trigger has seen them.  In such a case, any additional events
6622 * will result in creating new transition tables and allowing new firings of
6623 * statement triggers.
6624 *
6625 * This also saves the current event list location so that a later invocation
6626 * of this function can cheaply find the triggers we're about to queue and
6627 * cancel them.
6628 */
6629static void
6630cancel_prior_stmt_triggers(Oid relid, CmdType cmdType, int tgevent)
6631{
6632	AfterTriggersTableData *table;
6633	AfterTriggersQueryData *qs = &afterTriggers.query_stack[afterTriggers.query_depth];
6634
6635	/*
6636	 * We keep this state in the AfterTriggersTableData that also holds
6637	 * transition tables for the relation + operation.  In this way, if we are
6638	 * forced to make a new set of transition tables because more tuples get
6639	 * entered after we've already fired triggers, we will allow a new set of
6640	 * statement triggers to get queued without canceling the old ones.
6641	 */
6642	table = GetAfterTriggersTableData(relid, cmdType);
6643
6644	if (table->after_trig_done)
6645	{
6646		/*
6647		 * We want to start scanning from the tail location that existed just
6648		 * before we inserted any statement triggers.  But the events list
6649		 * might've been entirely empty then, in which case scan from the
6650		 * current head.
6651		 */
6652		AfterTriggerEvent event;
6653		AfterTriggerEventChunk *chunk;
6654
6655		if (table->after_trig_events.tail)
6656		{
6657			chunk = table->after_trig_events.tail;
6658			event = (AfterTriggerEvent) table->after_trig_events.tailfree;
6659		}
6660		else
6661		{
6662			chunk = qs->events.head;
6663			event = NULL;
6664		}
6665
6666		for_each_chunk_from(chunk)
6667		{
6668			if (event == NULL)
6669				event = (AfterTriggerEvent) CHUNK_DATA_START(chunk);
6670			for_each_event_from(event, chunk)
6671			{
6672				AfterTriggerShared evtshared = GetTriggerSharedData(event);
6673
6674				/*
6675				 * Exit loop when we reach events that aren't AS triggers for
6676				 * the target relation.
6677				 */
6678				if (evtshared->ats_relid != relid)
6679					goto done;
6680				if ((evtshared->ats_event & TRIGGER_EVENT_OPMASK) != tgevent)
6681					goto done;
6682				if (!TRIGGER_FIRED_FOR_STATEMENT(evtshared->ats_event))
6683					goto done;
6684				if (!TRIGGER_FIRED_AFTER(evtshared->ats_event))
6685					goto done;
6686				/* OK, mark it DONE */
6687				event->ate_flags &= ~AFTER_TRIGGER_IN_PROGRESS;
6688				event->ate_flags |= AFTER_TRIGGER_DONE;
6689			}
6690			/* signal we must reinitialize event ptr for next chunk */
6691			event = NULL;
6692		}
6693	}
6694done:
6695
6696	/* In any case, save current insertion point for next time */
6697	table->after_trig_done = true;
6698	table->after_trig_events = qs->events;
6699}
6700
6701/*
6702 * GUC assign_hook for session_replication_role
6703 */
6704void
6705assign_session_replication_role(int newval, void *extra)
6706{
6707	/*
6708	 * Must flush the plan cache when changing replication role; but don't
6709	 * flush unnecessarily.
6710	 */
6711	if (SessionReplicationRole != newval)
6712		ResetPlanCache();
6713}
6714
6715/*
6716 * SQL function pg_trigger_depth()
6717 */
6718Datum
6719pg_trigger_depth(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
6720{
6721	PG_RETURN_INT32(MyTriggerDepth);
6722}
6723
6724/*
6725 * Check whether a trigger modified a virtual generated column and replace the
6726 * value with null if so.
6727 *
6728 * We need to check this so that we don't end up storing a non-null value in a
6729 * virtual generated column.
6730 *
6731 * We don't need to check for stored generated columns, since those will be
6732 * overwritten later anyway.
6733 */
6734static HeapTuple
6735check_modified_virtual_generated(TupleDesc tupdesc, HeapTuple tuple)
6736{
6737	if (!(tupdesc->constr && tupdesc->constr->has_generated_virtual))
6738		return tuple;
6739
6740	for (int i = 0; i < tupdesc->natts; i++)
6741	{
6742		if (TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i)->attgenerated == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_VIRTUAL)
6743		{
6744			if (!heap_attisnull(tuple, i + 1, tupdesc))
6745			{
6746				int			replCol = i + 1;
6747				Datum		replValue = 0;
6748				bool		replIsnull = true;
6749
6750				tuple = heap_modify_tuple_by_cols(tuple, tupdesc, 1, &replCol, &replValue, &replIsnull);
6751			}
6752		}
6753	}
6754
6755	return tuple;
6756}
